                          V-254333 - Windows Server 2022 must prevent the display of slide shows on the lock screen.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254333r958478_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000010
                          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
                          Description: Slide shows that are displayed on the lock screen could display sensitive information to unauthorized personnel. Turning off this feature will limit access to the information to a logged-on user.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Control Panel >> Personalization >> Prevent enabling lock screen slide show to "Enabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254333
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000010 - Windows Server 2022 must prevent the display of slide shows on the lock screen.
                          Description:Slide shows that are displayed on the lock screen could display sensitive information to unauthorized personnel. Turning off this feature will limit access to the information to a logged-on user.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Verifies 'Prevent enabling lock screen slide show' is set to 'Enabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335200 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Verifies 'Prevent enabling lock screen slide show' is set to 'Enabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335200 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment:Registry object for HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization\NoLockScreenSlideshow
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization'
                          • name must be equal to 'NoLockScreenSlideshow'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Personalization\NoLockScreenSlideshow'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254334 - Windows Server 2022 must have WDigest Authentication disabled.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254334r958478_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000020
                          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
                          Description: When the WDigest Authentication protocol is enabled, plain-text passwords are stored in the Local Security Authority Subsystem Service (LSASS), exposing them to theft. WDigest is disabled by default in Windows Server 2022. This setting ensures this is enforced.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MS Security Guide . Set "WDigest Authentication (disabling may require KB2871997)" to "Disabled".

                          This policy setting requires the installation of the SecGuide custom templates included with the STIG package. "SecGuide.admx" and " SecGuide.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254334
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000020 - Windows Server 2022 must have WDigest Authentication disabled.
                          Description:When the WDigest Authentication protocol is enabled, plain-text passwords are stored in the Local Security Authority Subsystem Service (LSASS), exposing them to theft. WDigest is disabled by default in Windows Server 2022. This setting ensures this is enforced.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (WDigest Authentication is disabled)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335800 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:WDigest Authentication is disabled
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335800 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\Wdigest'
                          • name must be equal to 'UseLogonCredential'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\SecurityProviders\Wdigest\UseLogonCredential'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254339 - Windows Server 2022 insecure logons to an SMB server must be disabled.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254339r991589_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000070
                          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
                          Description: Insecure guest logons allow unauthenticated access to shared folders. Shared resources on a system must require authentication to establish proper access.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Network >> Lanman Workstation >> Enable insecure guest logons to "Disabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254339
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000070 - Windows Server 2022 insecure logons to an SMB server must be disabled.
                          Description:Insecure guest logons allow unauthenticated access to shared folders. Shared resources on a system must require authentication to establish proper access.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Enable insecure guest logons' is set to 'Disabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336000 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Enable insecure guest logons' is set to 'Disabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336000 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\LanmanWorkstation'
                          • name must be equal to 'AllowInsecureGuestAuth'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\LanmanWorkstation\AllowInsecureGuestAuth'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254340 - Windows Server 2022 hardened Universal Naming Convention (UNC) paths must be defined to require mutual authentication and integrity for at least the \\*\SYSVOL and \\*\NETLOGON shares.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254340r991589_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000080
                          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
                          Description: Additional security requirements are applied to UNC paths specified in hardened UNC paths before allowing access to them. This aids in preventing tampering with or spoofing of connections to these paths.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Network >> Network Provider >> Hardened UNC Paths" to "Enabled" with at least the following configured in "Hardened UNC Paths" (click the "Show" button to display):

                          Value Name: \\*\SYSVOL
                          Value: RequireMutualAuthentication=1, RequireIntegrity=1

                          Value Name: \\*\NETLOGON
                          Value: RequireMutualAuthentication=1, RequireIntegrity=1
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254340
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000080 - Windows Server 2022 hardened Universal Naming Convention (UNC) paths must be defined to require mutual authentication and integrity for at least the \*\SYSVOL and \*\NETLOGON shares.
                          Description:Additional security requirements are applied to UNC paths specified in hardened UNC paths before allowing access to them. This aids in preventing tampering with or spoofing of connections to these paths.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Mutual Authentication and Integrity for \\*\SYSVOL)
                              • false (Mutual Authentication and Integrity for \\*\NETLOGON)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336200 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Mutual Authentication and Integrity for \\*\SYSVOL
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          State Operator:All item-state comparisons must be true.
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336200 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment:Hardened Path Config for \\*\SYSVOL
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\NetworkProvider\HardenedPaths'
                          • name must be equal to '\\*\SYSVOL'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336200 (registry_state)
                          State Comment:RequireMutualAuthentication=1
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_sz'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must match the pattern '(^| *, *)RequireMutualAuthentication=1( *, *|$)'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336201 (registry_state)
                          State Comment:No RequireMutualAuthentication!=1
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_sz'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must match the pattern '(^|[ *, *])RequireMutualAuthentication=(?!1( *, *|$))'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336202 (registry_state)
                          State Comment:RequireIntegrity=1
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_sz'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must match the pattern '(^| *, *)RequireIntegrity=1( *, *|$)'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336203 (registry_state)
                          State Comment:No RequireIntegrity!=1
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_sz'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must match the pattern '(^| *, *)RequireIntegrity=(?!1( *, *|$))'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\NetworkProvider\HardenedPaths\\\*\SYSVOL'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336201 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Mutual Authentication and Integrity for \\*\NETLOGON
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          State Operator:All item-state comparisons must be true.
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336201 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment:Hardened Path Config for \\*\NETLOGON
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\NetworkProvider\HardenedPaths'
                          • name must be equal to '\\*\NETLOGON'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336200 (registry_state)
                          State Comment:RequireMutualAuthentication=1
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_sz'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must match the pattern '(^| *, *)RequireMutualAuthentication=1( *, *|$)'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336201 (registry_state)
                          State Comment:No RequireMutualAuthentication!=1
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_sz'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must match the pattern '(^|[ *, *])RequireMutualAuthentication=(?!1( *, *|$))'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336202 (registry_state)
                          State Comment:RequireIntegrity=1
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_sz'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must match the pattern '(^| *, *)RequireIntegrity=1( *, *|$)'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25336203 (registry_state)
                          State Comment:No RequireIntegrity!=1
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_sz'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must match the pattern '(^| *, *)RequireIntegrity=(?!1( *, *|$))'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\NetworkProvider\HardenedPaths\\\*\NETLOGON'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254341 - Windows Server 2022 command line data must be included in process creation events.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254341r958422_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000090
                          Identities:CCI-000135 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3 (1).1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 (1))
                          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

                          Enabling "Include command line data for process creation events" will record the command line information with the process creation events in the log. This can provide additional detail when malware has run on a system.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Audit Process Creation >> Include command line in process creation events to "Enabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254341
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000090 - Windows Server 2022 command line data must be included in process creation events.
                          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

                          Enabling "Include command line data for process creation events" will record the command line information with the process creation events in the log. This can provide additional detail when malware has run on a system.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Include command line in process creation events' is set to 'Enabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336700 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Include command line in process creation events' is set to 'Enabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336700 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\Audit'
                          • name must be equal (case insensitive) to 'ProcessCreationIncludeCmdLine_Enabled'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\Audit\ProcessCreationIncludeCmdLine_Enabled'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254342 - Windows Server 2022 must be configured to enable Remote host allows delegation of nonexportable credentials.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254342r991589_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000100
                          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
                          Description: An exportable version of credentials is provided to remote hosts when using credential delegation which exposes them to theft on the remote host. Restricted Admin mode or Remote Credential Guard allow delegation of nonexportable credentials providing additional protection of the credentials. Enabling this configures the host to support Restricted Admin mode or Remote Credential Guard.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Credentials Delegation >> Remote host allows delegation of nonexportable credentials to "Enabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254342
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000100 - Windows Server 2022 must be configured to enable Remote host allows delegation of nonexportable credentials.
                          Description:An exportable version of credentials is provided to remote hosts when using credential delegation which exposes them to theft on the remote host. Restricted Admin mode or Remote Credential Guard allow delegation of nonexportable credentials providing additional protection of the credentials. Enabling this configures the host to support Restricted Admin mode or Remote Credential Guard.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials' is set to 'Enabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25336800 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Remote host allows delegation of non-exportable credentials' is set to 'Enabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25336800 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CredentialsDelegation'
                          • name must be equal to 'AllowProtectedCreds'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\CredentialsDelegation\AllowProtectedCreds'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254343 - Windows Server 2022 virtualization-based security must be enabled with the platform security level configured to Secure Boot or Secure Boot with DMA Protection.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254343r991589_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer_or_DomainController
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000110
                          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
                          Description: Virtualization Based Security (VBS) provides the platform for the additional security features Credential Guard and virtualization-based protection of code integrity. Secure Boot is the minimum security level, with DMA protection providing additional memory protection. DMA Protection requires a CPU that supports input/output memory management unit (IOMMU).
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Device Guard >> Turn On Virtualization Based Security to "Enabled" with "Secure Boot" or "Secure Boot and DMA Protection" selected.

                          A Microsoft TechNet article on Credential Guard, including system requirement details, can be found at the following link:

                          https://technet.microsoft.com/itpro/windows/keep-secure/credential-guard
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:def:2101
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:SRG-OS-000480-GPOS-00227, SV-254343r991589
                          Description:Windows Server 2022 virtualization-based security must be enabled with the platform security level configured to Secure Boot or Secure Boot with DMA Protection.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (Windows Server virtualization-based security must be enabled with the platform security level configured to Secure Boot or Secure Boot with DMA Protection. RequiredSecurityProperties)
                            • true (Windows Server virtualization-based security must be enabled with the platform security level configured to Secure Boot or Secure Boot with DMA Protection. VirtualizationBasedSecurityStatus)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:tst:2101 (cmdlet_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Windows Server virtualization-based security must be enabled with the platform security level configured to Secure Boot or Secure Boot with DMA Protection. RequiredSecurityProperties
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:One and only one item may match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:obj:2101 (cmdlet_object)
                          Object Comment:DeviceGuard RequiredSecurityProperties
                          Object Requirements:
                          • module_name must be equal to 'Microsoft.Powershell.Management'
                          • verb must be equal to 'Get'
                          • noun must be equal to 'CimInstance'
                          • for 'parameters' the following must be true:
                            • classname must be equal to 'Win32_DeviceGuard'
                            • namespace must be equal to 'root\Microsoft\Windows\DeviceGuard'
                          • for 'select' the following must be true:
                            • property must be equal to 'RequiredSecurityProperties'
                          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:ste:2101 (cmdlet_state)
                          State Comment:requiredsecurityproperties includes 2
                          State Requirements:
                          • for only one 'value' the following must be true:
                            • all requiredsecurityproperties must match the pattern '2'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • module_name equals 'Microsoft.PowerShell.Management'
                          • module_id equals '{eefcb906-b326-4e99-9f54-8b4bb6ef3c6d}'
                          • module_version equals '3.1.0.0'
                          • verb equals 'Get'
                          • noun equals 'CimInstance'
                          • collected 'parameters' result:
                            • classname = 'Win32_DeviceGuard'
                            • namespace = 'root\Microsoft\Windows\DeviceGuard'
                          • collected 'select' result:
                            • property = 'RequiredSecurityProperties'
                          • collected 'value' result:
                            • requiredsecurityproperties = '{0}'
                          Result Analysis:requiredsecurityproperties = '{0}' failed test of 'pattern match' compared to 2
                          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:tst:2102 (cmdlet_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:Windows Server virtualization-based security must be enabled with the platform security level configured to Secure Boot or Secure Boot with DMA Protection. VirtualizationBasedSecurityStatus
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:One and only one item may match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:obj:2102 (cmdlet_object)
                          Object Comment:DeviceGuard VirtualizationBasedSecurityStatus
                          Object Requirements:
                          • module_name must be equal to 'Microsoft.Powershell.Management'
                          • verb must be equal to 'Get'
                          • noun must be equal to 'CimInstance'
                          • for 'parameters' the following must be true:
                            • classname must be equal to 'Win32_DeviceGuard'
                            • namespace must be equal to 'root\Microsoft\Windows\DeviceGuard'
                          • for 'select' the following must be true:
                            • property must be equal to 'VirtualizationBasedSecurityStatus'
                          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:ste:2102 (cmdlet_state)
                          State Comment:virtualizationbasedsecuritystatus equals 2
                          State Requirements:
                          • for only one 'value' the following must be true:
                            • all virtualizationbasedsecuritystatus must be equal to '2'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • module_name equals 'Microsoft.PowerShell.Management'
                          • module_id equals '{eefcb906-b326-4e99-9f54-8b4bb6ef3c6d}'

                          V-254344 - Windows Server 2022 Early Launch Antimalware, Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy must prevent boot drivers identified as bad.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254344r991589_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-CC-000130
                          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
                          Description: Compromised boot drivers can introduce malware prior to protection mechanisms that load after initialization. The Early Launch Antimalware driver can limit allowed drivers based on classifications determined by the malware protection application. At a minimum, drivers determined to be bad must not be allowed.
                          Fix Text:The default behavior is for Early Launch Antimalware - Boot-Start Driver Initialization policy to enforce "Good, unknown and bad but critical" (preventing "bad").

                          If this needs to be corrected or a more secure setting is desired, configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Early Launch Antimalware >> Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy to "Not Configured" or "Enabled" with any option other than "All" selected.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254344
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-CC-000130 - Windows Server 2022 Early Launch Antimalware, Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy must prevent boot drivers identified as bad.
                          Description:Compromised boot drivers can introduce malware prior to protection mechanisms that load after initialization. The Early Launch Antimalware driver can limit allowed drivers based on classifications determined by the malware protection application. At a minimum, drivers determined to be bad must not be allowed.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true ('Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy' is not set to 'Enabled' with 'All' selected)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25434400 (registry_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:'Boot-Start Driver Initialization Policy' is not set to 'Enabled' with 'All' selected
                          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25337200 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Policies\EarlyLaunch'
                          • name must be equal to 'DriverLoadPolicy'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000003 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '3'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25337200 (registry_state)
                          State Comment:Reg_Dword type and value equals 8
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '8'

                          V-254345 - Windows Server 2022 group policy objects must be reprocessed even if they have not changed.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254345r991589_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000140
                          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
                          Description: Registry entries for group policy settings can potentially be changed from the required configuration. This could occur as part of troubleshooting or by a malicious process on a compromised system. Enabling this setting and then selecting the "Process even if the Group Policy objects have not changed" option ensures the policies will be reprocessed even if none have been changed. This way, any unauthorized changes are forced to match the domain-based group policy settings again.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Group Policy >> Configure registry policy processing to "Enabled" with the option "Process even if the Group Policy objects have not changed" selected.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254345
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000140 - Windows Server 2022 group policy objects must be reprocessed even if they have not changed.
                          Description:Registry entries for group policy settings can potentially be changed from the required configuration. This could occur as part of troubleshooting or by a malicious process on a compromised system. Enabling this setting and then selecting the "Process even if the Group Policy objects have not changed" option ensures the policies will be reprocessed even if none have been changed. This way, any unauthorized changes are forced to match the domain-based group policy settings again.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (NoGPOistChanges is set to 0.)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25337300 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:NoGPOistChanges is set to 0.
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25337300 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Group Policy\{35378EAC-683F-11D2-A89A-00C04FBBCFA2}'
                          • name must be equal to 'NoGPOListChanges'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Group Policy\{35378EAC-683F-11D2-A89A-00C04FBBCFA2}\NoGPOListChanges'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254346 - Windows Server 2022 downloading print driver packages over HTTP must be turned off.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254346r958478_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000150
                          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
                          Description: Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and will prevent uncontrolled updates to the system.

                          This setting prevents the computer from downloading print driver packages over HTTP.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Internet Communication Management >> Internet Communication settings >> Turn off downloading of print drivers over HTTP to "Enabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254346
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000150 - Windows Server 2022 downloading print driver packages over HTTP must be turned off.
                          Description:Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and will prevent uncontrolled updates to the system.

                          This setting prevents the computer from downloading print driver packages over HTTP.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Turn off downloading of print drivers over HTTP' is set to 'Enabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25337400 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Turn off downloading of print drivers over HTTP' is set to 'Enabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25337400 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Printers'
                          • name must be equal to 'DisableWebPnPDownload'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Printers\DisableWebPnPDownload'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254347 - Windows Server 2022 printing over HTTP must be turned off.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254347r958478_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000160
                          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
                          Description: Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and will prevent uncontrolled updates to the system.

                          This setting prevents the client computer from printing over HTTP, which allows the computer to print to printers on the intranet as well as the internet.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Internet Communication Management >> Internet Communication settings >> Turn off printing over HTTP to "Enabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254347
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000160 - Windows Server 2022 printing over HTTP must be turned off.
                          Description:Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Turning off this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise and will prevent uncontrolled updates to the system.

                          This setting prevents the client computer from printing over HTTP, which allows the computer to print to printers on the intranet as well as the internet.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Turn off printing over HTTP' is set to 'Enabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25337600 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Turn off printing over HTTP' is set to 'Enabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25337600 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Printers'
                          • name must be equal to 'DisableHTTPPrinting'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Printers\DisableHTTPPrinting'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254348 - Windows Server 2022 network selection user interface (UI) must not be displayed on the logon screen.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254348r958478_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000170
                          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
                          Description: Enabling interaction with the network selection UI allows users to change connections to available networks without signing in to Windows.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Logon >> Do not display network selection UI to "Enabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254348
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000170 - Windows Server 2022 network selection user interface (UI) must not be displayed on the logon screen.
                          Description:Enabling interaction with the network selection UI allows users to change connections to available networks without signing in to Windows.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Verifies 'Do not display network selection UI' is set to 'Enabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25337800 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Verifies 'Do not display network selection UI' is set to 'Enabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25337800 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment:HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System\DontDisplayNetworkSelectionUI
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System'
                          • name must be equal to 'DontDisplayNetworkSelectionUI'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System\DontDisplayNetworkSelectionUI'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254349 - Windows Server 2022 users must be prompted to authenticate when the system wakes from sleep (on battery).

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254349r991589_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000180
                          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
                          Description: A system that does not require authentication when resuming from sleep may provide access to unauthorized users. Authentication must always be required when accessing a system. This setting ensures users are prompted for a password when the system wakes from sleep (on battery).
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Power Management >> Sleep Settings >> Require a password when a computer wakes (on battery) to "Enabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254349
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000180 - Windows Server 2022 users must be prompted to authenticate when the system wakes from sleep (on battery).
                          Description:A system that does not require authentication when resuming from sleep may provide access to unauthorized users. Authentication must always be required when accessing a system. This setting ensures users are prompted for a password when the system wakes from sleep (on battery).
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Require a password when a computer wakes (on battery)' is set to 'Enabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25338000 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Require a password when a computer wakes (on battery)' is set to 'Enabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25338000 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Power\PowerSettings\0e796bdb-100d-47d6-a2d5-f7d2daa51f51'
                          • name must be equal to 'DCSettingIndex'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Power\PowerSettings\0e796bdb-100d-47d6-a2d5-f7d2daa51f51\DCSettingIndex'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254350 - Windows Server 2022 users must be prompted to authenticate when the system wakes from sleep (plugged in).

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254350r991589_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000190
                          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
                          Description: A system that does not require authentication when resuming from sleep may provide access to unauthorized users. Authentication must always be required when accessing a system. This setting ensures users are prompted for a password when the system wakes from sleep (plugged in).
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Power Management >> Sleep Settings >> Require a password when a computer wakes (plugged in) to "Enabled".

                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.

                          • key must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Power\PowerSettings\0e796bdb-100d-47d6-a2d5-f7d2daa51f51'
                          • name must be equal to 'ACSettingIndex'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)

                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Power\PowerSettings\0e796bdb-100d-47d6-a2d5-f7d2daa51f51\ACSettingIndex'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254355 - Windows Server 2022 administrator accounts must not be enumerated during elevation.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254355r958518_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000240
                          Identities:CCI-001084 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-3.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-3)
                          Description: Enumeration of administrator accounts when elevating can provide part of the logon information to an unauthorized user. This setting configures the system to always require users to type in a username and password to elevate a running application.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Credential User Interface >> Enumerate administrator accounts on elevation to "Disabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254355
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000240 - Windows Server 2022 administrator accounts must not be enumerated during elevation.
                          Description:Enumeration of administrator accounts when elevating can provide part of the logon information to an unauthorized user. This setting configures the system to always require users to type in a username and password to elevate a running application.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Enumerate administrator accounts on elevation' is set to 'Disabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25339100 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Enumerate administrator accounts on elevation' is set to 'Disabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25339100 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\CredUI'
                          • name must be equal to 'EnumerateAdministrators'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\CredUI\EnumerateAdministrators'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254356 - Windows Server 2022 Diagnostic Data must be configured to send "required diagnostic data" or "optional diagnostic data".

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254356r991589_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000250
                          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
                          Description: Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Limiting this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise. The "send required diagnostic data" option for Allow Diagnostic Data configures the lowest amount of data, effectively none outside of the Malicious Software Removal Tool (MSRT), Defender, and Diagnostic Data client settings. "Optional Diagnostic Data" sends basic diagnostic and usage data and may be required to support some Microsoft services.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Data Collection and Preview Build >> Allow Diagnostic Data to "Enabled" with "Send required diagnostic data" selected or "Send optional diagnostic data".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254356
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000250 - Windows Server 2022 Telemetry must be configured to Security or Basic.
                          Description:Some features may communicate with the vendor, sending system information or downloading data or components for the feature. Limiting this capability will prevent potentially sensitive information from being sent outside the enterprise. The "Security" option for Telemetry configures the lowest amount of data, effectively none outside of the Malicious Software Removal Tool (MSRT), Defender, and telemetry client settings. "Basic" sends basic diagnostic and usage data and may be required to support some Microsoft services.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Allow Diagnostic Data' is set to 'Enabled' with 'Send Required Diagnostic Data' or 'Send Optional Diagnostic Data' selected in 'Options:')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25435600 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Allow Diagnostic Data' is set to 'Enabled' with 'Send Required Diagnostic Data' or 'Send Optional Diagnostic Data' selected in 'Options:'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25339300 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection'
                          • name must be equal to 'AllowTelemetry'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000003 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '3'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\DataCollection\AllowTelemetry'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254358 - Windows Server 2022 Application event log size must be configured to 32768 KB or greater.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254358r958752_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000270
                          Identities:CCI-001849 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-4; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-4)
                          Description: Inadequate log size will cause the log to fill up quickly. This may prevent audit events from being recorded properly and require frequent attention by administrative personnel.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Event Log Service >> Application >> Specify the maximum log file size (KB) to "Enabled" with a "Maximum Log Size (KB)" of "32768" or greater.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254358
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000270 - Windows Server 2022 Application event log size must be configured to 32768 KB or greater.
                          Description:Inadequate log size will cause the log to fill up quickly. This may prevent audit events from being recorded properly and require frequent attention by administrative personnel.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (The Application event log size is configured to 32768 KB or greater)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25333700 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:The Application event log size is configured to 32768 KB or greater
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25333700 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Application'
                          • name must be equal (case insensitive) to 'MaxSize'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25333700 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be greater than or equal to '32768'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Application\MaxSize'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          • key must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\EventLog\Security'

                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be greater than or equal to '196608'
