                          V-254361 - Windows Server 2022 Microsoft Defender antivirus SmartScreen must be enabled.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254361r958478_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000300
                          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
                          Description: Microsoft Defender antivirus SmartScreen helps protect systems from programs downloaded from the internet that may be malicious. Enabling SmartScreen can block potentially malicious programs or warn users.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> File Explorer >> Configure Windows Defender SmartScreen to "Enabled" with either option "Warn" or "Warn and prevent bypass" selected.

                          Windows Server 2022 includes duplicate policies for this setting. It can also be configured under Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Defender SmartScreen >> Explorer.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254361
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000300 - Windows Server 2022 Microsoft Defender antivirus SmartScreen must be enabled.
                          Description:Microsoft Defender antivirus SmartScreen helps protect systems from programs downloaded from the internet that may be malicious. Enabling SmartScreen can block potentially malicious programs or warn users.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Configure Windows SmartScreen' is set to 'Enabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25339500 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Configure Windows SmartScreen' is set to 'Enabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25339500 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System'
                          • name must be equal to 'EnableSmartScreen'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System\EnableSmartScreen'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254362 - Windows Server 2022 Explorer Data Execution Prevention must be enabled.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254362r958928_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-CC-000310
                          Identities:CCI-002824 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SI-16; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SI-16)
                          Description: Data Execution Prevention provides additional protection by performing checks on memory to help prevent malicious code from running. This setting will prevent Data Execution Prevention from being turned off for File Explorer.
                          Fix Text:The default behavior is for data execution prevention to be turned on for File Explorer.

                          If this needs to be corrected, configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> File Explorer >> Turn off Data Execution Prevention for Explorer to "Not Configured" or "Disabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254362
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-CC-000310 - Windows Server 2022 Explorer Data Execution Prevention must be enabled.
                          Description:Data Execution Prevention provides additional protection by performing checks on memory to help prevent malicious code from running. This setting will prevent Data Execution Prevention from being turned off for File Explorer.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true ('Turn off Data Execution Prevention for Explorer' is set to 'Disabled' or 'Not Configured')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25339600 (registry_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:'Turn off Data Execution Prevention for Explorer' is set to 'Disabled' or 'Not Configured'
                          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25339600 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Explorer'
                          • name must be equal to 'NoDataExecutionPrevention'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'

                          V-254364 - Windows Server 2022 File Explorer shell protocol must run in protected mode.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254364r991589_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-CC-000330
                          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
                          Description: The shell protocol will limit the set of folders that applications can open when run in protected mode. Restricting files an application can open to a limited set of folders increases the security of Windows.
                          Fix Text:The default behavior is for shell protected mode to be turned on for File Explorer.

                          If this needs to be corrected, configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> File Explorer >> Turn off shell protocol protected mode to "Not Configured" or "Disabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254364
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-CC-000330 - Windows Server 2022 File Explorer shell protocol must run in protected mode.
                          Description:The shell protocol will limit the set of folders that applications can open when run in protected mode. Restricting files an application can open to a limited set of folders increases the security of Windows.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true ('Turn off shell protocol protected mode' is set to 'Disabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25339800 (registry_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:'Turn off shell protocol protected mode' is set to 'Disabled'
                          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25339800 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Explorer'
                          • name must be equal to 'PreXPSP2ShellProtocolBehavior'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'

                          V-254365 - Windows Server 2022 must not save passwords in the Remote Desktop Client.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254365r1050789_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000340
                          Identities:CCI-004895 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-11 b)
                          CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11)
                          CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11)
                          Description: Saving passwords in the Remote Desktop Client could allow an unauthorized user to establish a remote desktop session to another system. The system must be configured to prevent users from saving passwords in the Remote Desktop Client.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00156, SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00157
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Remote Desktop Services >> Remote Desktop Connection Client >> Do not allow passwords to be saved to "Enabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254365
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000340 - Windows Server 2022 must not save passwords in the Remote Desktop Client.
                          Description:Saving passwords in the Remote Desktop Client could allow an unauthorized user to establish a remote desktop session to another system. The system must be configured to prevent users from saving passwords in the Remote Desktop Client.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00156, SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00157
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Do not allow passwords to be saved' is set to 'Enabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25340200 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Do not allow passwords to be saved' is set to 'Enabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25340200 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services'
                          • name must be equal to 'DisablePasswordSaving'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services\DisablePasswordSaving'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254366 - Windows Server 2022 Remote Desktop Services must prevent drive redirection.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254366r958524_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000350
                          Identities:CCI-001090 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-4; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-4.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-4; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-4)
                          Description: Preventing users from sharing the local drives on their client computers with Remote Session Hosts that they access helps reduce possible exposure of sensitive data.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Remote Desktop Services >> Remote Desktop Session Host >> Device and Resource Redirection >> Do not allow drive redirection to "Enabled".

                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:

                          Description:Preventing users from sharing the local drives on their client computers with Remote Session Hosts that they access helps reduce possible exposure of sensitive data.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Do not allow drive redirection' is set to 'Enabled')

                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.

                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25340300 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services'
                          • name must be equal to 'fDisableCdm'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment:

                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services\fDisableCdm'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254367 - Windows Server 2022 Remote Desktop Services must always prompt a client for passwords upon connection.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254367r1050789_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000360
                          Identities:CCI-004895 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-11 b)
                          CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11)
                          CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11)
                          Description: This setting controls the ability of users to supply passwords automatically as part of their remote desktop connection. Disabling this setting would allow anyone to use the stored credentials in a connection item to connect to the terminal server.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00156, SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00157
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Remote Desktop Services >> Remote Desktop Session Host >> Security >> Always prompt for password upon connection to "Enabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254367
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000360 - Windows Server 2022 Remote Desktop Services must always prompt a client for passwords upon connection.
                          Description:This setting controls the ability of users to supply passwords automatically as part of their remote desktop connection. Disabling this setting would allow anyone to use the stored credentials in a connection item to connect to the terminal server.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00156, SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00157
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Always prompt for password upon connection' is set to 'Enabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25340400 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Always prompt for password upon connection' is set to 'Enabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25340400 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services'
                          • name must be equal to 'fPromptForPassword'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services\fPromptForPassword'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254368 - Windows Server 2022 Remote Desktop Services must require secure Remote Procedure Call (RPC) communications.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254368r958408_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000370
                          Identities:CCI-000068 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-17 (2); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-17 (2).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-17 (2); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-17 (2))
                          CCI-001453 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-17 (2); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-17 (2).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-17 (2); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-17 (2))
                          Description: Allowing unsecure RPC communication exposes the system to man-in-the-middle attacks and data disclosure attacks. A man-in-the-middle attack occurs when an intruder captures packets between a client and server and modifies them before allowing the packets to be exchanged. Usually the attacker will modify the information in the packets in an attempt to cause either the client or server to reveal sensitive information.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000033-GPOS-00014, SRG-OS-000250-GPOS-00093
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Remote Desktop Services >> Remote Desktop Session Host >> Security >> Require secure RPC communication to "Enabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254368
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000370 - Windows Server 2022 Remote Desktop Services must require secure Remote Procedure Call (RPC) communications.
                          Description:Allowing unsecure RPC communication exposes the system to man-in-the-middle attacks and data disclosure attacks. A man-in-the-middle attack occurs when an intruder captures packets between a client and server and modifies them before allowing the packets to be exchanged. Usually the attacker will modify the information in the packets in an attempt to cause either the client or server to reveal sensitive information.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000033-GPOS-00014, SRG-OS-000250-GPOS-00093
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Require secure RPC communication' is set to 'Enabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25340500 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Require secure RPC communication' is set to 'Enabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25340500 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services'
                          • name must be equal to 'fEncryptRPCTraffic'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services\fEncryptRPCTraffic'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254369 - Windows Server 2022 Remote Desktop Services must be configured with the client connection encryption set to High Level.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254369r958408_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000380
                          Identities:CCI-000068 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-17 (2); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-17 (2).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-17 (2); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-17 (2))
                          CCI-001453 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-17 (2); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-17 (2).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-17 (2); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-17 (2))
                          Description: Remote connections must be encrypted to prevent interception of data or sensitive information. Selecting "High Level" will ensure encryption of Remote Desktop Services sessions in both directions.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000033-GPOS-00014, SRG-OS-000250-GPOS-00093
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Remote Desktop Services >> Remote Desktop Session Host >> Security >> Set client connection encryption level to "Enabled" with "High Level" selected.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254369
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000380 - Windows Server 2022 Remote Desktop Services must be configured with the client connection encryption set to High Level.
                          Description:Remote connections must be encrypted to prevent interception of data or sensitive information. Selecting "High Level" will ensure encryption of Remote Desktop Services sessions in both directions.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000033-GPOS-00014, SRG-OS-000250-GPOS-00093
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Set client connection encryption level' is set to 'Enabled' and 'High Level')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25340600 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Set client connection encryption level' is set to 'Enabled' and 'High Level'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25340600 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services'
                          • name must be equal to 'MinEncryptionLevel'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000003 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '3'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Terminal Services\MinEncryptionLevel'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254370 - Windows Server 2022 must prevent attachments from being downloaded from RSS feeds.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254370r991589_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000390
                          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
                          Description: Attachments from RSS feeds may not be secure. This setting will prevent attachments from being downloaded from RSS feeds.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> RSS Feeds >> Prevent downloading of enclosures to "Enabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0

                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:

                              • false ('Prevent downloading of enclosures' is set to 'Enabled')

                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Feeds'
                          • name must be equal to 'DisableEnclosureDownload'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)

                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Feeds\DisableEnclosureDownload'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254371 - Windows Server 2022 must disable Basic authentication for RSS feeds over HTTP.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254371r958478_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-CC-000400
                          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
                          Description: Basic authentication uses plain-text passwords that could be used to compromise a system. Disabling Basic authentication will reduce this potential.
                          Fix Text:The default behavior is for the Windows RSS platform to not use Basic authentication over HTTP connections.

                          If this needs to be corrected, configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> RSS Feeds >> Turn on Basic feed authentication over HTTP to "Not Configured" or "Disabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254371
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-CC-000400 - Windows Server 2022 must disable Basic authentication for RSS feeds over HTTP.
                          Description:Basic authentication uses plain-text passwords that could be used to compromise a system. Disabling Basic authentication will reduce this potential.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true ('Turn on Basic feed authentication over HTTP' is set to 'Not Configured' or 'Disabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25340800 (registry_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:'Turn on Basic feed authentication over HTTP' is set to 'Not Configured' or 'Disabled'
                          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25340800 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Internet Explorer\Feeds'
                          • name must be equal to 'AllowBasicAuthInClear'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'

                          V-254372 - Windows Server 2022 must prevent Indexing of encrypted files.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254372r958478_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000410
                          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
                          Description: Indexing of encrypted files may expose sensitive data. This setting prevents encrypted files from being indexed.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Search >> Allow indexing of encrypted files to "Disabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254372
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000410 - Windows Server 2022 must prevent Indexing of encrypted files.
                          Description:Indexing of encrypted files may expose sensitive data. This setting prevents encrypted files from being indexed.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Allow indexing of encrypted files' is set to 'Disabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25340900 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Allow indexing of encrypted files' is set to 'Disabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25340900 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search'
                          • name must be equal to 'AllowIndexingEncryptedStoresOrItems'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Search\AllowIndexingEncryptedStoresOrItems'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254373 - Windows Server 2022 must prevent users from changing installation options.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254373r1016483_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000420
                          Identities:CCI-003980 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-11 (2))
                          CCI-001812 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-11 (2))
                          CCI-001812 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-11 (2))
                          Description: Installation options for applications are typically controlled by administrators. This setting prevents users from changing installation options that may bypass security features.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Installer >> Allow user control over installs to "Disabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254373
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000420 - Windows Server 2022 must prevent users from changing installation options.
                          Description:Installation options for applications are typically controlled by administrators. This setting prevents users from changing installation options that may bypass security features.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Allow user control over installs' is set to 'Disabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25341000 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Allow user control over installs' is set to 'Disabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25341000 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Installer'
                          • name must be equal to 'EnableUserControl'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Installer\EnableUserControl'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254375 - Windows Server 2022 users must be notified if a web-based program attempts to install software.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254375r991589_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-CC-000440
                          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
                          Description: Web-based programs may attempt to install malicious software on a system. Ensuring users are notified if a web-based program attempts to install software allows them to refuse the installation.
                          Fix Text:The default behavior is for Internet Explorer to warn users and select whether to allow or refuse installation when a web-based program attempts to install software on the system.

                          If this needs to be corrected, configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Installer >> Prevent Internet Explorer security prompt for Windows Installer scripts to "Not Configured" or "Disabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254375
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-CC-000440 - Windows Server 2022 users must be notified if a web-based program attempts to install software.
                          Description:Web-based programs may attempt to install malicious software on a system. Ensuring users are notified if a web-based program attempts to install software allows them to refuse the installation.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true ('Prevent Internet Explorer security prompt for Windows Installer scripts' is set to 'Not Configured' or 'Disabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25341200 (registry_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:'Prevent Internet Explorer security prompt for Windows Installer scripts' is set to 'Not Configured' or 'Disabled'
                          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25341200 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Installer'
                          • name must be equal to 'SafeForScripting'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'

                          V-254376 - Windows Server 2022 must disable automatically signing in the last interactive user after a system-initiated restart.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254376r991591_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-CC-000450
                          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
                          Description: Windows can be configured to automatically sign the user back in after a Windows Update restart. Some protections are in place to help ensure this is done in a secure fashion; however, disabling this will prevent the caching of credentials for this purpose and also ensure the user is aware of the restart.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Logon Options >> Sign-in and lock last interactive user automatically after a restart to "Disabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254376
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-CC-000450 - Windows Server 2022 must disable automatically signing in the last interactive user after a system-initiated restart.
                          Description:Windows can be configured to automatically sign the user back in after a Windows Update restart. Some protections are in place to help ensure this is done in a secure fashion; however, disabling this will prevent the caching of credentials for this purpose and also ensure the user is aware of the restart.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true (Verifies 'Sign-in last interactive user automatically after a system-initiated restart' is set to 'Disabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25341300 (registry_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:Verifies 'Sign-in last interactive user automatically after a system-initiated restart' is set to 'Disabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25341300 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
                          • name must be equal to 'DisableAutomaticRestartSignOn'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
                          • name equals 'DisableAutomaticRestartSignOn'
                          • last_write_time equals '134244553680000000'
                          • type equals 'reg_dword'
                          • value equals '1'
                          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

                          V-254377 - Windows Server 2022 PowerShell script block logging must be enabled.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254377r958422_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000460
                          Identities:CCI-000135 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3 (1); NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3 (1).1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 (1))
                          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

                          Enabling PowerShell script block logging will record detailed information from the processing of PowerShell commands and scripts. This can provide additional detail when malware has run on a system.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows PowerShell >> Turn on PowerShell Script Block Logging to "Enabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254377
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000460 - Windows Server 2022 PowerShell script block logging must be enabled.
                          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

                          Enabling PowerShell script block logging will record detailed information from the processing of PowerShell commands and scripts. This can provide additional detail when malware has run on a system.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Turn on PowerShell Script Block Logging' is set to 'Enabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25341400 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Turn on PowerShell Script Block Logging' is set to 'Enabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25341400 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\ScriptBlockLogging'
                          • name must be equal (case insensitive) to 'EnableScriptBlockLogging'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\ScriptBlockLogging\EnableScriptBlockLogging'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254379 - Windows Server 2022 Windows Remote Management (WinRM) client must not allow unencrypted traffic.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254379r958848_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000480
                          Identities:CCI-002890 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: MA-4 (6); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: MA-4 (6))
                          CCI-003123 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: MA-4 (6); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: MA-4 (6))
                          Description: Unencrypted remote access to a system can allow sensitive information to be compromised. Windows remote management connections must be encrypted to prevent this.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000393-GPOS-00173, SRG-OS-000394-GPOS-00174
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Remote Management (WinRM) >> WinRM Client >> Allow unencrypted traffic to "Disabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254379
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000480 - Windows Server 2022 Windows Remote Management (WinRM) client must not allow unencrypted traffic.
                          Description:Unencrypted remote access to a system can allow sensitive information to be compromised. Windows remote management connections must be encrypted to prevent this.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000393-GPOS-00173, SRG-OS-000394-GPOS-00174
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Allow unencrypted traffic' is set to 'Disabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25341700 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Allow unencrypted traffic' is set to 'Disabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25341700 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WinRM\Client'
                          • name must be equal to 'AllowUnencryptedTraffic'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WinRM\Client\AllowUnencryptedTraffic'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254380 - Windows Server 2022 Windows Remote Management (WinRM) client must not use Digest authentication.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254380r958510_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000490
                          Identities:CCI-000877 (NIST SP 800-53: MA-4 c; NIST SP 800-53A: MA-4.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: MA-4 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: MA-4 c)
                          Description: Digest authentication is not as strong as other options and may be subject to man-in-the-middle attacks. Disallowing Digest authentication will reduce this potential.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Remote Management (WinRM) >> WinRM Client >> Disallow Digest authentication to "Enabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022

                          Type:DPMS Target

                          Title:WN22-CC-000490 - Windows Server 2022 Windows Remote Management (WinRM) client must not use Digest authentication.
                          Description:Digest authentication is not as strong as other options and may be subject to man-in-the-middle attacks. Disallowing Digest authentication will reduce this potential.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Disallow Digest authentication' is set to 'Enabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25342100 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Disallow Digest authentication' is set to 'Enabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25342100 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WinRM\Client'
                          • name must be equal to 'AllowDigest'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WinRM\Client\AllowDigest'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
