                          V-254383 - Windows Server 2022 Windows Remote Management (WinRM) service must not store RunAs credentials.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254383r1050789_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000520
                          Identities:CCI-004895 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-11 b)
                          CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11)
                          CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11)
                          Description: Storage of administrative credentials could allow unauthorized access. Disallowing the storage of RunAs credentials for Windows Remote Management will prevent them from being used with plug-ins.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00156, SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00157
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows Remote Management (WinRM) >> WinRM Service >> Disallow WinRM from storing RunAs credentials to "Enabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254383
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000520 - Windows Server 2022 Windows Remote Management (WinRM) service must not store RunAs credentials.
                          Description:Storage of administrative credentials could allow unauthorized access. Disallowing the storage of RunAs credentials for Windows Remote Management will prevent them from being used with plug-ins.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00156, SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00157
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Disallow WinRM from storing RunAs credentials' is set to 'Enabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25342000 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Disallow WinRM from storing RunAs credentials' is set to 'Enabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25342000 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WinRM\Service'
                          • name must be equal to 'DisableRunAs'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\WinRM\Service\DisableRunAs'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254384 - Windows Server 2022 must have PowerShell Transcription enabled.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254384r958420_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-CC-000530
                          Identities:CCI-000134 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-3 e)
                          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

                          Enabling PowerShell Transcription will record detailed information from the processing of PowerShell commands and scripts. This can provide additional detail when malware has run on a system.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Windows PowerShell >> "Turn on PowerShell Transcription" to "Enabled".

                          Specify the Transcript output directory to point to a Central Log Server or another secure location to prevent user access.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254384
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-CC-000530 - Windows Server 2022 must have PowerShell Transcription enabled.
                          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

                          Enabling PowerShell Transcription will record detailed information from the processing of PowerShell commands and scripts. This can provide additional detail when malware has run on a system.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (PowerShell Transcription is enabled on Windows 11)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25341500 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:PowerShell Transcription is enabled on Windows 11
                          Check Existence:All collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25341500 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\Transcription'
                          • name must be equal to 'EnableTranscripting'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\PowerShell\Transcription\EnableTranscripting'. Existence requirement is 'all_exist' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254386 - Windows Server 2022 Kerberos user logon restrictions must be enforced.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254386r1016486_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-DC-000020
                          Identities:CCI-001941 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (8); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-2 (8))
                          CCI-001942 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (9))
                          CCI-001942 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (9))
                          Description: This policy setting determines whether the Kerberos Key Distribution Center (KDC) validates every request for a session ticket against the user rights policy of the target computer. The policy is enabled by default, which is the most secure setting for validating that access to target resources is not circumvented.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000112-GPOS-00057, SRG-OS-000113-GPOS-00058
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value in the Default Domain Policy for Computer Configuration >> Policies >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Kerberos Policy >> Enforce user logon restrictions to "Enabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254386
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-DC-000020 - Windows Server 2022 Kerberos user logon restrictions must be enforced.
                          Description:This policy setting determines whether the Kerberos Key Distribution Center (KDC) validates every request for a session ticket against the user rights policy of the target computer. The policy is enabled by default, which is the most secure setting for validating that access to target resources is not circumvented.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000112-GPOS-00057, SRG-OS-000113-GPOS-00058
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Kerberos user logon restrictions must be enforced, PA test = -1 OR Other tools test = 1)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25438600 (wmi57_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Kerberos user logon restrictions must be enforced, PA test = -1 OR Other tools test = 1
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25438600 (wmi57_object)
                          Object Comment:Kerberos Policy - Enforce user logon restrictions
                          Object Requirements:
                          • namespace must be equal to 'root\rsop\computer'
                          • wql must be equal to 'SELECT setting FROM RSOP_SecuritySettingBoolean WHERE KeyName='TicketValidateClient''
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25438600 (wmi57_state)
                          State Comment:Kerberos Policy - Enforce user logon restrictions - Enabled, PA test = -1
                          State Requirements:
                          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
                            • all setting must be equal to '-1'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25438601 (wmi57_state)
                          State Comment:Kerberos Policy - Enforce user logon restrictions - Enabled, Other tools test = 1
                          State Requirements:
                          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
                            • all setting must be equal to '1'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ does not exist ]
                          • namespace equals 'root\rsop\computer'
                          • wql equals 'SELECT setting FROM RSOP_SecuritySettingBoolean WHERE KeyName='TicketValidateClient''
                          Result Analysis:. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
                          .

                          V-254387 - Windows Server 2022 Kerberos service ticket maximum lifetime must be limited to 600 minutes or less.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254387r1016487_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-DC-000030
                          Identities:CCI-001941 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (8); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-2 (8))
                          CCI-001942 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (9))
                          CCI-001942 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (9))
                          Description: This setting determines the maximum amount of time (in minutes) that a granted session ticket can be used to access a particular service. Session tickets are used only to authenticate new connections with servers. Ongoing operations are not interrupted if the session ticket used to authenticate the connection expires during the connection.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000112-GPOS-00057, SRG-OS-000113-GPOS-00058
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value in the Default Domain Policy for Computer Configuration >> Policies >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Kerberos Policy >> Maximum lifetime for service ticket to a maximum of "600" minutes, but not "0", which equates to "Ticket doesn't expire".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254387
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-DC-000030 - Windows Server 2022 Kerberos service ticket maximum lifetime must be limited to 600 minutes or less.
                          Description:This setting determines the maximum amount of time (in minutes) that a granted session ticket can be used to access a particular service. Session tickets are used only to authenticate new connections with servers. Ongoing operations are not interrupted if the session ticket used to authenticate the connection expires during the connection.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000112-GPOS-00057, SRG-OS-000113-GPOS-00058
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Kerberos Policy - Maximum lifetime for service ticket - 600 minutes or less and greater than 0)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25438700 (wmi57_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Kerberos Policy - Maximum lifetime for service ticket - 600 minutes or less and greater than 0
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          State Operator:All item-state comparisons must be true.
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25438700 (wmi57_object)
                          Object Comment:Kerberos Policy - Maximum lifetime for service ticket must be 600 or less
                          Object Requirements:
                          • namespace must be equal to 'root\rsop\computer'
                          • wql must be equal to 'SELECT setting FROM RSOP_SecuritySettingNumeric WHERE KeyName='MaxServiceAge''
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25438700 (wmi57_state)
                          State Comment:Maximum lifetime for service ticket is less than or eequal to 600
                          State Requirements:
                          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
                            • all setting must be less than or equal to '600'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25438701 (wmi57_state)
                          State Comment:Maximum lifetime for service ticket policy is greater than 0.
                          State Requirements:
                          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
                            • all setting must be greater than '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ does not exist ]
                          • namespace equals 'root\rsop\computer'
                          • wql equals 'SELECT setting FROM RSOP_SecuritySettingNumeric WHERE KeyName='MaxServiceAge''
                          Result Analysis:. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
                          .

                          V-254388 - Windows Server 2022 Kerberos user ticket lifetime must be limited to 10 hours or less.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254388r1016488_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-DC-000040
                          Identities:CCI-001941 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (8); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-2 (8))
                          CCI-001942 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (9))
                          CCI-001942 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (9))
                          Description: In Kerberos, there are two types of tickets: Ticket Granting Tickets (TGTs) and Service Tickets. Kerberos tickets have a limited lifetime so the time an attacker has to implement an attack is limited. This policy controls how long TGTs can be renewed. With Kerberos, the user's initial authentication to the domain controller results in a TGT, which is then used to request Service Tickets to resources. Upon startup, each computer gets a TGT before requesting a service ticket to the domain controller and any other computers it needs to access. For services that start up under a specified user account, users must always get a TGT first and then get Service Tickets to all computers and services accessed.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000112-GPOS-00057, SRG-OS-000113-GPOS-00058
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value in the Default Domain Policy for Computer Configuration >> Policies >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Kerberos Policy >> Maximum lifetime for user ticket to a maximum of "10" hours but not "0", which equates to "Ticket doesn't expire".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254388
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-DC-000040 - Windows Server 2022 Kerberos user ticket lifetime must be limited to 10 hours or less.
                          Description:In Kerberos, there are two types of tickets: Ticket Granting Tickets (TGTs) and Service Tickets. Kerberos tickets have a limited lifetime so the time an attacker has to implement an attack is limited. This policy controls how long TGTs can be renewed. With Kerberos, the user's initial authentication to the domain controller results in a TGT, which is then used to request Service Tickets to resources. Upon startup, each computer gets a TGT before requesting a service ticket to the domain controller and any other computers it needs to access. For services that start up under a specified user account, users must always get a TGT first and then get Service Tickets to all computers and services accessed.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000112-GPOS-00057, SRG-OS-000113-GPOS-00058
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Kerberos Policy - Maximum lifetime for user ticket - 10 hours or less, but greater than 0)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25438800 (wmi57_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Kerberos Policy - Maximum lifetime for user ticket - 10 hours or less, but greater than 0
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          State Operator:All item-state comparisons must be true.
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25438800 (wmi57_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • namespace must be equal to 'root\rsop\computer'
                          • wql must be equal to 'SELECT setting FROM RSOP_SecuritySettingNumeric WHERE KeyName='MaxTicketAge''
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25438800 (wmi57_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
                            • all setting must be less than or equal to '10'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25438801 (wmi57_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
                            • all setting must be greater than '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ does not exist ]
                          • namespace equals 'root\rsop\computer'
                          • wql equals 'SELECT setting FROM RSOP_SecuritySettingNumeric WHERE KeyName='MaxTicketAge''
                          Result Analysis:. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
                          .

                          V-254389 - Windows Server 2022 Kerberos policy user ticket renewal maximum lifetime must be limited to seven days or less.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254389r1016489_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-DC-000050
                          Identities:CCI-001941 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (8); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-2 (8))
                          CCI-001942 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (9))
                          CCI-001942 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (9))
                          Description: This setting determines the period of time (in days) during which a user's Ticket Granting Ticket (TGT) may be renewed. This security configuration limits the amount of time an attacker has to crack the TGT and gain access.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000112-GPOS-00057, SRG-OS-000113-GPOS-00058
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value in the Default Domain Policy for Computer Configuration >> Policies >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Kerberos Policy >> Maximum lifetime for user ticket renewal to a maximum of "7" days or less.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254389
                          Definition Result:false

                          Description:This setting determines the period of time (in days) during which a user's Ticket Granting Ticket (TGT) may be renewed. This security configuration limits the amount of time an attacker has to crack the TGT and gain access.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000112-GPOS-00057, SRG-OS-000113-GPOS-00058
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Kerberos Policy - Maximum lifetime for user ticket renewal - 7 days or less)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25438900 (wmi57_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Kerberos Policy - Maximum lifetime for user ticket renewal - 7 days or less

                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25438900 (wmi57_object)
                          Object Comment:Kerberos Policy - Maximum lifetime for user ticket renewal must be 7 days or less
                          Object Requirements:
                          • namespace must be equal to 'root\rsop\computer'
                          • wql must be equal to 'SELECT setting FROM RSOP_SecuritySettingNumeric WHERE KeyName='MaxRenewAge''
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25438900 (wmi57_state)
                          State Comment:Maximum lifetime for user ticket renewal lte 7
                          State Requirements:
                          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
                            • all setting must be less than or equal to '7'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ does not exist ]
                          • namespace equals 'root\rsop\computer'
                          • wql equals 'SELECT setting FROM RSOP_SecuritySettingNumeric WHERE KeyName='MaxRenewAge''
                          Result Analysis:. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
                          .

                          V-254390 - Windows Server 2022 computer clock synchronization tolerance must be limited to five minutes or less.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254390r1016490_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-DC-000060
                          Identities:CCI-001941 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (8); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-2 (8))
                          CCI-001942 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (9))
                          CCI-001942 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2 (9))
                          Description: This setting determines the maximum time difference (in minutes) that Kerberos will tolerate between the time on a client's clock and the time on a server's clock while still considering the two clocks synchronous. To prevent replay attacks, Kerberos uses timestamps as part of its protocol definition. For timestamps to work properly, the clocks of the client and the server need to be in sync as much as possible.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000112-GPOS-00057, SRG-OS-000113-GPOS-00058
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value in the Default Domain Policy for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Account Policies >> Kerberos Policy >> Maximum tolerance for computer clock synchronization to a maximum of "5" minutes or less.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254390
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-DC-000060 - Windows Server 2022 computer clock synchronization tolerance must be limited to five minutes or less.
                          Description:This setting determines the maximum time difference (in minutes) that Kerberos will tolerate between the time on a client's clock and the time on a server's clock while still considering the two clocks synchronous. In order to prevent replay attacks, Kerberos uses timestamps as part of its protocol definition. For timestamps to work properly, the clocks of the client and the server need to be in sync as much as possible.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000112-GPOS-00057, SRG-OS-000113-GPOS-00058
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Kerberos Policy - Maximum tolerance for computer clock synchronization - 5 minutes or less)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25439000 (wmi57_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Kerberos Policy - Maximum tolerance for computer clock synchronization - 5 minutes or less
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25439000 (wmi57_object)
                          Object Comment:Kerberos Policy - Maximum tolerance for computer clock synchronization must be 5 minutes or less
                          Object Requirements:
                          • namespace must be equal to 'root\rsop\computer'
                          • wql must be equal to 'SELECT setting FROM RSOP_SecuritySettingNumeric WHERE KeyName='MaxClockSkew''
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25439000 (wmi57_state)
                          State Comment:Maximum tolerance for computer clock synchronization lte 5
                          State Requirements:
                          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
                            • all setting must be less than or equal to '5'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ does not exist ]
                          • namespace equals 'root\rsop\computer'
                          • wql equals 'SELECT setting FROM RSOP_SecuritySettingNumeric WHERE KeyName='MaxClockSkew''
                          Result Analysis:. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
                          .

                          V-254407 - Windows Server 2022 must be configured to audit Account Management - Computer Account Management successes.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254407r958368_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-DC-000230
                          Identities:CCI-000018 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-2 (4).1 (i and ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-2 (4))
                          CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
                          CCI-001403 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-2 (4).1 (i and ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-2 (4))
                          CCI-001404 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-2 (4).1 (i and ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-2 (4))
                          CCI-001405 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53A: AC-2 (4).1 (i and ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-2 (4))
                          CCI-002130 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-2 (4); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-2 (4))
                          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

                          Computer Account Management records events such as creating, changing, deleting, renaming, disabling, or enabling computer accounts.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000004-GPOS-00004, SRG-OS-000239-GPOS-00089, SRG-OS-000240-GPOS-00090, SRG-OS-000241-GPOS-00091, SRG-OS-000303-GPOS-00120, SRG-OS-000476-GPOS-00221
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> Account Management >> Audit Computer Account Management with Success selected.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254407
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-DC-000230 - Windows Server 2022 must be configured to audit Account Management - Computer Account Management successes.
                          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

                          Computer Account Management records events such as creating, changing, deleting, renaming, disabling, or enabling computer accounts.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000004-GPOS-00004, SRG-OS-000239-GPOS-00089, SRG-OS-000240-GPOS-00090, SRG-OS-000241-GPOS-00091, SRG-OS-000303-GPOS-00120, SRG-OS-000476-GPOS-00221
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true ('Computer Account Management' is set to 'Success' or 'Success' and 'Failure')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25440700 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:'Computer Account Management' is set to 'Success' or 'Success' and 'Failure'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000000 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25440700 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
                          State Comment:'Computer Account Management' is set to 'Success'
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', computer_account_management must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25440701 (auditeventpolicysubcategories_state)
                          State Comment:'Computer Account Management' is set to 'Success' and 'Failure'
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', computer_account_management must be equal to 'AUDIT_SUCCESS_FAILURE'

                          [ true ]

                          V-254408 - Windows Server 2022 must be configured to audit DS Access - Directory Service Access successes.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254408r958732_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-DC-000240
                          Identities:CCI-000172 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 c; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 c)
                          CCI-002234 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (9); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (9))
                          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

                          Audit Directory Service Access records events related to users accessing an Active Directory object.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000458-GPOS-00203, SRG-OS-000463-GPOS-00207, SRG-OS-000468-GPOS-00212
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Advanced Audit Policy Configuration >> System Audit Policies >> DS Access >> Directory Service Access with Success selected.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254408
                          Definition Result:true

                          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised.                           Audit Directory Service Access records events related to users accessing an Active Directory object.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000327-GPOS-00127, SRG-OS-000458-GPOS-00203, SRG-OS-000463-GPOS-00207, SRG-OS-000468-GPOS-00212

                          V-254410 - Windows Server 2022 must be configured to audit DS Access - Directory Service Changes successes.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254410r958732_rule

                          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior.

                          Audit Directory Service Changes records events related to changes made to objects in Active Directory Domain Services.

                          State Comment:

                          V-254416 - Windows Server 2022 domain controllers must require LDAP access signing.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254416r958908_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-DC-000320
                          Identities:CCI-002418 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-8; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-8)
                          CCI-002421 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-8 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-8 (1))
                          Description: Unsigned network traffic is susceptible to man-in-the-middle attacks, where an intruder captures packets between the server and the client and modifies them before forwarding them to the client. In the case of an LDAP server, this means that an attacker could cause a client to make decisions based on false records from the LDAP directory. The risk of an attacker pulling this off can be decreased by implementing strong physical security measures to protect the network infrastructure. Furthermore, implementing Internet Protocol security (IPsec) authentication header mode (AH), which performs mutual authentication and packet integrity for Internet Protocol (IP) traffic, can make all types of man-in-the-middle attacks extremely difficult.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000423-GPOS-00187, SRG-OS-000424-GPOS-00188
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> Domain controller: LDAP server signing requirements to "Require signing".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254416
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-DC-000320 - Windows Server 2022 domain controllers must require LDAP access signing.
                          Description:Unsigned network traffic is susceptible to man-in-the-middle attacks, where an intruder captures packets between the server and the client and modifies them before forwarding them to the client. In the case of an LDAP server, this means that an attacker could cause a client to make decisions based on false records from the LDAP directory. The risk of an attacker pulling this off can be decreased by implementing strong physical security measures to protect the network infrastructure. Furthermore, implementing Internet Protocol security (IPsec) authentication header mode (AH), which performs mutual authentication and packet integrity for Internet Protocol (IP) traffic, can make all types of man-in-the-middle attacks extremely difficult.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000423-GPOS-00187, SRG-OS-000424-GPOS-00188
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Domain Controller: LDAP Server signing requirements' is set to 'Require signing')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25441600 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Domain Controller: LDAP Server signing requirements' is set to 'Require signing'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25441600 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'System\CurrentControlSet\Services\NTDS\Parameters'
                          • name must be equal to 'LDAPServerIntegrity'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000002 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '2'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\NTDS\Parameters\LDAPServerIntegrity'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254417 - Windows Server 2022 domain controllers must be configured to allow reset of machine account passwords.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254417r991589_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-DC-000330
                          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
                          Description: Enabling this setting on all domain controllers in a domain prevents domain members from changing their computer account passwords. If these passwords are weak or compromised, the inability to change them may leave these computers vulnerable.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> Domain controller: Refuse machine account password changes to "Disabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254417
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-DC-000330 - Windows Server 2022 domain controllers must be configured to allow reset of machine account passwords.
                          Description:Enabling this setting on all domain controllers in a domain prevents domain members from changing their computer account passwords. If these passwords are weak or compromised, the inability to change them may leave these computers vulnerable.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Domain controller: Refuse machine account password changes' is set to 'Disabled'.)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25441700 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Domain controller: Refuse machine account password changes' is set to 'Disabled'.
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25441700 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netlogon\Parameters'
                          • name must be equal (case insensitive) to 'RefusePasswordChange'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netlogon\Parameters\RefusePasswordChange'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254418 - Windows Server 2022 Access this computer from the network user right must only be assigned to the Administrators, Authenticated Users, and Enterprise Domain Controllers groups on domain controllers.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254418r958472_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-DC-000340
                          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Access this computer from the network" right may access resources on the system, and this right must be limited to those requiring it.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Access this computer from the network to include only the following accounts or groups:

                          - Administrators
                          - Authenticated Users
                          - Enterprise Domain Controllers
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254418
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-DC-000340 - Windows Server 2022 Access this computer from the network user right must only be assigned to the Administrators, Authenticated Users, and
                          Enterprise Domain Controllers groups on domain controllers.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Access this computer from the network" right may access resources on the system, and this right must be limited to those requiring it.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Access this computer from the network - Administrators, Authenticated Users, Enterprise Domain Controllers)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25441800 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Access this computer from the network - Administrators, Authenticated Users, Enterprise Domain Controllers
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25441800 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment:accounts or groups other than Administrators, Authenticated Users and Enterprise Domain Controllers
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Authenticated Users'
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Enterprise Domain Controllers'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25348000 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', senetworklogonright must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • senetworklogonright equals '1' for: Users, Backup Operators, Everyone
                          • senetworklogonright equals '0' for NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, RDS Endpoint Servers, Guests, Performance Log Users, Guest, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, IUSR, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, Remote Management Users, TERMINAL SERVER USER, DIALUP, ANONYMOUS LOGON, DefaultAccount, Remote Desktop Users, Distributed COM Users, SYSTEM, NETWORK, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, PROXY, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, Replicator, Access Control Assistance Operators, Hyper-V Administrators, Performance Monitor Users, Storage Replica Administrators, WDAGUtilityAccount, RDS Management Servers, Event Log Readers, SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, Network Configuration Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Administrator, Device Owners, Cryptographic Operators, Print Operators, IIS_IUSRS, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, System Managed Accounts Group, INTERACTIVE, CREATOR OWNER, RDS Remote Access Servers, OpenSSH Users, User Mode Hardware Operators, NETWORK SERVICE
                          Result Analysis:senetworklogonright : Users = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'
                          senetworklogonright : Backup Operators = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'
                          senetworklogonright : Everyone = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'

                          V-254419 - Windows Server 2022 Add workstations to domain user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group on domain controllers.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254419r958726_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-DC-000350
                          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Add workstations to domain" right may add computers to a domain. This could result in unapproved or incorrectly configured systems being added to a domain.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Add workstations to domain to include only the following accounts or groups:

                          - Administrators
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254419
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-DC-000350 - Windows Server 2022 Add workstations to domain user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group on domain controllers.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Add workstations to domain" right may add computers to a domain. This could result in unapproved or incorrectly configured systems being added to a domain.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true (Add workstations to domain (SeMachineAccountPrivilege) - Administrators)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25441900 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:Add workstations to domain (SeMachineAccountPrivilege) - Administrators
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000009 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment:Administrators
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25441900 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Add workstations to domain (SeMachineAccountPrivilege)
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', semachineaccountprivilege must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • semachineaccountprivilege equals '1' for:
                          • semachineaccountprivilege equals '0' for NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Authenticated Users, RDS Endpoint Servers, Guests, Users, Performance Log Users, Guest, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, IUSR, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, Remote Management Users, TERMINAL SERVER USER, DIALUP, ANONYMOUS LOGON, DefaultAccount, Remote Desktop Users, Distributed COM Users, SYSTEM, NETWORK, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, PROXY, Backup Operators, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, Replicator, Access Control Assistance Operators, Hyper-V Administrators, Performance Monitor Users, Storage Replica Administrators, Everyone, WDAGUtilityAccount, RDS Management Servers, Event Log Readers, SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, Network Configuration Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Administrator, Device Owners, Cryptographic Operators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, Print Operators, IIS_IUSRS, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, System Managed Accounts Group, INTERACTIVE, CREATOR OWNER, RDS Remote Access Servers, OpenSSH Users, User Mode Hardware Operators, NETWORK SERVICE

                          V-254420 - Windows Server 2022 Allow log on through Remote Desktop Services user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group on domain controllers.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254420r958472_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-DC-000360
                          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Allow log on through Remote Desktop Services" user right can access a system through Remote Desktop.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Allow log on through Remote Desktop Services to include only the following accounts or groups:

                          - Administrators

                          Reference:

                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target

                          Title:WN22-DC-000360 - Windows Server 2022 Allow log on through Remote Desktop Services user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group on domain controllers.

                          Accounts with the "Allow log on through Remote Desktop Services" user right can access a system through Remote Desktop.
