                          V-254421 - Windows Server 2022 Deny access to this computer from the network user right on domain controllers must be configured to prevent unauthenticated access.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254421r958472_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-DC-000370
                          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Deny access to this computer from the network" user right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on from the network.

                          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Deny access to this computer from the network to include the following:

                          - Guests Group
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254421
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-DC-000370 - Windows Server 2022 Deny access to this computer from the network user right on domain controllers must be configured to prevent unauthenticated access.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Deny access to this computer from the network" user right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on from the network.

                          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Deny access to this computer from the network - Guests)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349100 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Deny access to this computer from the network - Guests
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000006 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • security_principle must be equal (case insensitive) to 'Guests'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349100 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Deny access to this computer from the network
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenynetworklogonright must be equal to '1'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '1' for:
                          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '0' for Guests
                          Result Analysis:sedenynetworklogonright : Guests = '0' failed test of 'equals' compared to '1'

                          V-254422 - Windows Server 2022 Deny log on as a batch job user right on domain controllers must be configured to prevent unauthenticated access.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254422r958472_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-DC-000380
                          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Deny log on as a batch job" user right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on to the system as a batch job, such as Task Scheduler.

                          The Guests group must be assigned to prevent unauthenticated access.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Deny log on as a batch job to include the following:

                          - Guests Group
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254422
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-DC-000380 - Windows Server 2022 Deny log on as a batch job user right on domain controllers must be configured to prevent unauthenticated access.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Deny log on as a batch job" user right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on to the system as a batch job, such as Task Scheduler.

                          The Guests group must be assigned to prevent unauthenticated access.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Deny log on as a batch job is assigned to Guests)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25442200 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Deny log on as a batch job is assigned to Guests
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000006 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • security_principle must be equal (case insensitive) to 'Guests'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349200 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenybatchLogonright must be equal to '1'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • sedenybatchLogonright equals '1' for:
                          • sedenybatchLogonright equals '0' for Guests
                          Result Analysis:sedenybatchLogonright : Guests = '0' failed test of 'equals' compared to '1'

                          V-254423 - Windows Server 2022 Deny log on as a service user right must be configured to include no accounts or groups (blank) on domain controllers.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254423r958472_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-DC-000390
                          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Deny log on as a service" user right defines accounts that are denied logon as a service.

                          Incorrect configurations could prevent services from starting and result in a denial of service.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Deny log on as a service to include no entries (blank).
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254423
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-DC-000390 - Windows Server 2022 Deny log on as a service user right must be configured to include no accounts or groups (blank) on domain controllers.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Deny log on as a service" user right defines accounts that are denied logon as a service.

                          Incorrect configurations could prevent services from starting and result in a denial of service.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true (Deny log on as a service - None)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25442300 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:Deny log on as a service - None
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000008 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment:All security principles
                          Object Requirements:
                          • security_principle must match the pattern '.+'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25442300 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Deny log on as a service
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenyservicelogonright must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • sedenyservicelogonright equals '1' for:
                          • sedenyservicelogonright equals '0' for Access Control Assistance Operators, Administrators, Backup Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, Cryptographic Operators, Device Owners, Distributed COM Users, Event Log Readers, Guests, Hyper-V Administrators, IIS_IUSRS, Network Configuration Operators, OpenSSH Users, Performance Log Users, Performance Monitor Users, Power Users, Print Operators, RDS Endpoint Servers, RDS Management Servers, RDS Remote Access Servers, Remote Desktop Users, Remote Management Users, Replicator, Storage Replica Administrators, System Managed Accounts Group, User Mode Hardware Operators, Users, Administrator, DefaultAccount, Guest, WDAGUtilityAccount, NETWORK SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, SYSTEM, IUSR, TERMINAL SERVER USER, Authenticated Users, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, PROXY, ANONYMOUS LOGON, SERVICE, INTERACTIVE, BATCH, NETWORK, DIALUP, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, CREATOR GROUP, CREATOR OWNER, Everyone, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Window Manager\Window Manager Group

                          V-254424 - Windows Server 2022 Deny log on locally user right on domain controllers must be configured to prevent unauthenticated access.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254424r958472_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-DC-000400
                          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Deny log on locally" user right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on interactively.

                          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Deny log on locally to include the following:

                          - Guests Group
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:

                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-DC-000400 - Windows Server 2022 Deny log on locally user right on domain controllers must be configured to prevent unauthenticated access.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Deny log on locally" user right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on interactively.

                          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Deny log on locally - Guests)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349400 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Deny log on locally - Guests
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000006 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • security_principle must be equal (case insensitive) to 'Guests'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349400 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Deny log on locally
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenyinteractivelogonright must be equal to '1'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • sedenyinteractivelogonright equals '1' for:
                          • sedenyinteractivelogonright equals '0' for Guests
                          Result Analysis:sedenyinteractivelogonright : Guests = '0' failed test of 'equals' compared to '1'

                          V-254425 - Windows Server 2022 Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services user right on domain controllers must be configured to prevent unauthenticated access.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254425r958672_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-DC-000410
                          Identities:CCI-002314 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-17 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-17 (1))
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" user right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on using Remote Desktop Services.

                          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services to include the following:

                          - Guests Group
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0

                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" user right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on using Remote Desktop Services.

                          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
                          Class:compliance

                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000006 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • security_principle must be equal (case insensitive) to 'Guests'

                          State Comment:Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services
                          State Requirements:

                          Collected Item/State Result:

                          • sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright equals '0' for Guests

                          V-254426 - Windows Server 2022 Enable computer and user accounts to be trusted for delegation user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group on domain controllers.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254426r958726_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_DomainController
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-DC-000420
                          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Enable computer and user accounts to be trusted for delegation" user right allows the "Trusted for Delegation" setting to be changed. This could allow unauthorized users to impersonate other users.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Enable computer and user accounts to be trusted for delegation to include only the following accounts or groups:

                          - Administrators
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254426
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-DC-000420 - Windows Server 2022 Enable computer and user accounts to be trusted for delegation user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group on domain controllers.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Enable computer and user accounts to be trusted for delegation" user right allows the "Trusted for Delegation" setting to be changed. This could allow unauthorized users to impersonate other users.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true (Enable computer and user accounts to be trusted for delegation - Administrators)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25442600 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:Enable computer and user accounts to be trusted for delegation - Administrators
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000009 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment:Administrators
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349600 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Enable computer and user accounts to be trusted for delegation
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', seenabledelegationprivilege must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • seenabledelegationprivilege equals '1' for:
                          • seenabledelegationprivilege equals '0' for NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Authenticated Users, RDS Endpoint Servers, Guests, Users, Performance Log Users, Guest, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, IUSR, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, Remote Management Users, TERMINAL SERVER USER, DIALUP, ANONYMOUS LOGON, DefaultAccount, Remote Desktop Users, Distributed COM Users, SYSTEM, NETWORK, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, PROXY, Backup Operators, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, Replicator, Access Control Assistance Operators, Hyper-V Administrators, Performance Monitor Users, Storage Replica Administrators, Everyone, WDAGUtilityAccount, RDS Management Servers, Event Log Readers, SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, Network Configuration Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Administrator, Device Owners, Cryptographic Operators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, Print Operators, IIS_IUSRS, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, System Managed Accounts Group, INTERACTIVE, CREATOR OWNER, RDS Remote Access Servers, OpenSSH Users, User Mode Hardware Operators, NETWORK SERVICE

                          V-254429 - Windows Server 2022 local administrator accounts must have their privileged token filtered to prevent elevated privileges from being used over the network on domain-joined member servers.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254429r958518_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-MS-000020
                          Identities:CCI-001084 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-3.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-3)
                          Description: A compromised local administrator account can provide means for an attacker to move laterally between domain systems.

                          With User Account Control enabled, filtering the privileged token for local administrator accounts will prevent the elevated privileges of these accounts from being used over the network.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> MS Security Guide >> Apply UAC restrictions to local accounts on network logons to "Enabled".

                          This policy setting requires the installation of the SecGuide custom templates included with the STIG package. "SecGuide.admx" and " SecGuide.adml" must be copied to the \Windows\PolicyDefinitions and \Windows\PolicyDefinitions\en-US directories respectively.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254429
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-MS-000020 - Windows Server 2022 local administrator accounts must have their privileged token filtered to prevent elevated privileges from being used over the network on domain-joined member servers.
                          Description:A compromised local administrator account can provide means for an attacker to move laterally between domain systems.

                          With User Account Control enabled, filtering the privileged token for local administrator accounts will prevent the elevated privileges of these accounts from being used over the network.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Local admin accounts filtered token policy is enabled)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25335700 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Local admin accounts filtered token policy is enabled
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25335700 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
                          • name must be equal to 'LocalAccountTokenFilterPolicy'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25335700 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\LocalAccountTokenFilterPolicy'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254430 - Windows Server 2022 local users on domain-joined member servers must not be enumerated.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254430r958478_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-MS-000030
                          Identities:CCI-000381 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-7; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-7.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 a)
                          Description: The username is one part of logon credentials that could be used to gain access to a system. Preventing the enumeration of users limits this information to authorized personnel.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Logon >> Enumerate local users on domain-joined computers to "Disabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254430
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-MS-000030 - Windows Server 2022 local users on domain-joined member servers must not be enumerated.
                          Description:The username is one part of logon credentials that could be used to gain access to a system. Preventing the enumeration of users limits this information to authorized personnel.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Enumerate local users on domain-joined computers' is set to 'Disabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25337900 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Enumerate local users on domain-joined computers' is set to 'Disabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25337900 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System'
                          • name must be equal to 'EnumerateLocalUsers'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\System\EnumerateLocalUsers'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254431 - Windows Server 2022 must restrict unauthenticated Remote Procedure Call (RPC) clients from connecting to the RPC server on domain-joined member servers and standalone or nondomain-joined systems.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254431r971545_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_StandaloneServer
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-MS-000040
                          Identities:CCI-001967 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-3 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-3 (1))
                          Description: Unauthenticated RPC clients may allow anonymous access to sensitive information. Configuring RPC to restrict unauthenticated RPC clients from connecting to the RPC server will prevent anonymous connections.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> Remote Procedure Call >> Restrict Unauthenticated RPC clients to "Enabled" with "Authenticated" selected.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254431
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-MS-000040 - Windows Server 2022 must restrict unauthenticated Remote Procedure Call (RPC) clients from connecting to the RPC server on domain-joined member servers and standalone or nondomain-joined systems.
                          Description:Unauthenticated RPC clients may allow anonymous access to sensitive information. Configuring RPC to restrict unauthenticated RPC clients from connecting to the RPC server will prevent anonymous connections.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Restrict Unauthenticated RPC clients' is set to 'Enabled' and 'Authenticated')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25338300 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Restrict Unauthenticated RPC clients' is set to 'Enabled' and 'Authenticated'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25338300 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Rpc'
                          • name must be equal to 'RestrictRemoteClients'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Windows NT\Rpc\RestrictRemoteClients'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254432 - Windows Server 2022 must limit the caching of logon credentials to four or less on domain-joined member servers.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254432r991589_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-MS-000050
                          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
                          Description: The default Windows configuration caches the last logon credentials for users who log on interactively to a system. This feature is provided for system availability reasons, such as the user's machine being disconnected from the network or domain controllers being unavailable. Even though the credential cache is well protected, if a system is attacked, an unauthorized individual may isolate the password to a domain user account using a password-cracking program and gain access to the domain.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> Interactive Logon: Number of previous logons to cache (in case Domain Controller is not available) to "4" logons or less.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254432
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-MS-000050 - Windows Server 2022 must limit the caching of logon credentials to four or less on domain-joined member servers.
                          Description:The default Windows configuration caches the last logon credentials for users who log on interactively to a system. This feature is provided for system availability reasons, such as the user's machine being disconnected from the network or domain controllers being unavailable. Even though the credential cache is well protected, if a system is attacked, an unauthorized individual may isolate the password to a domain user account using a password-cracking program and gain access to the domain.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Interactive logon: Number of previous logons to cache' is set to 4 or less)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25443200 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Interactive logon: Number of previous logons to cache' is set to 4 or less
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25344700 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon'
                          • name must be equal to 'CachedLogonsCount'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25443200 (registry_state)
                          State Comment:Reg_sz value of 4 or less
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_sz'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be less than or equal to '4'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon'
                          • name equals 'CachedLogonsCount'
                          • last_write_time equals '134244551010000000'
                          • type equals 'reg_sz'
                          • value equals '10'
                          • windows_view equals '64_bit'
                          Result Analysis:HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\CachedLogonsCount\value = '10' failed test of 'less than or equal' compared to '4'

                          V-254433 - Windows Server 2022 must restrict remote calls to the Security Account Manager (SAM) to Administrators on domain-joined member servers and standalone or nondomain-joined systems.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254433r958726_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_StandaloneServer
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-MS-000060
                          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
                          Description: The Windows SAM stores users' passwords. Restricting Remote Procedure Call (RPC) connections to the SAM to Administrators helps protect those credentials.
                          Fix Text:Navigate to the policy Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> Network access: Restrict clients allowed to make remote calls to SAM.

                          Select "Edit Security" to configure the "Security descriptor:".

                          Add "Administrators" in "Group or user names:" if it is not already listed (this is the default).

                          Select "Administrators" in "Group or user names:".

                          Select "Allow" for "Remote Access" in "Permissions for "Administrators".

                          Click "OK".

                          The "Security descriptor:" must be populated with "O:BAG:BAD:(A;;RC;;;BA) for the policy to be enforced.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254433
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-MS-000060 - Windows Server 2022 must restrict remote calls to the Security Account Manager (SAM) to Administrators on domain-joined member servers and standalone or nondomain-joined systems.
                          Description:The Windows SAM stores users' passwords. Restricting Remote Procedure Call (RPC) connections to the SAM to Administrators helps protect those credentials.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Allow Administrators group to make remote calls to SAM)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows:tst:25345700 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Allow Administrators group to make remote calls to SAM
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows:obj:25345700 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
                          • name must be equal to 'RestrictRemoteSAM'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows:ste:25345700 (registry_state)
                          State Comment:Reg_Sz type and value equals O:BAG:BAD:(A;;RC;;;BA)
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_sz'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to 'O:BAG:BAD:(A;;RC;;;BA)'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\RestrictRemoteSAM'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254434 - Windows Server 2022 Access this computer from the network user right must only be assigned to the Administrators and Authenticated Users groups on domain-joined member servers and standalone or nondomain-joined systems.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254434r958472_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_StandaloneServer
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-MS-000070
                          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Access this computer from the network" user right may access resources on the system, and this right must be limited to those requiring it.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Access this computer from the network to include only the following accounts or groups:

                          - Administrators
                          - Authenticated Users
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254434
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-MS-000070 - Windows Server 2022 Access this computer from the network user right must only be assigned to the Administrators and Authenticated Users groups on domain-joined member servers and standalone or nondomain-joined systems.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Access this computer from the network" user right may access resources on the system, and this right must be limited to those requiring it.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (The 'Access this computer from the network to include only the following accounts or groups' user right is assigned only to Administrators and Authenticated Users)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25443400 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:The 'Access this computer from the network to include only the following accounts or groups' user right is assigned only to Administrators and Authenticated Users
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25443400 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Authenticated Users'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25348000 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', senetworklogonright must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • senetworklogonright equals '1' for: Users, Backup Operators, Everyone
                          • senetworklogonright equals '0' for NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, RDS Endpoint Servers, Guests, Performance Log Users, Guest, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, IUSR, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, Remote Management Users, TERMINAL SERVER USER, DIALUP, ANONYMOUS LOGON, DefaultAccount, Remote Desktop Users, Distributed COM Users, SYSTEM, NETWORK, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, PROXY, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, Replicator, Access Control Assistance Operators, Hyper-V Administrators, Performance Monitor Users, Storage Replica Administrators, WDAGUtilityAccount, RDS Management Servers, Event Log Readers, SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, Network Configuration Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Administrator, Device Owners, Cryptographic Operators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, Print Operators, IIS_IUSRS, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, System Managed Accounts Group, INTERACTIVE, CREATOR OWNER, RDS Remote Access Servers, OpenSSH Users, User Mode Hardware Operators, NETWORK SERVICE
                          Result Analysis:senetworklogonright : Users = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'
                          senetworklogonright : Backup Operators = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'
                          senetworklogonright : Everyone = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'

                          V-254435 - Windows Server 2022 Deny access to this computer from the network user right on domain-joined member servers must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and local accounts and from unauthenticated access on all systems.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254435r958472_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_StandaloneServer
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-MS-000080
                          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Deny access to this computer from the network" user right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on from the network.

                          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower-trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

                          Local accounts on domain-joined systems must also be assigned this right to decrease the risk of lateral movement resulting from credential theft attacks.

                          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Deny access to this computer from the network to include the following:

                          Domain Systems Only:
                          - Enterprise Admins group
                          - Domain Admins group
                          - "Local account and member of Administrators group" or "Local account" (see Note below)

                          All Systems:
                          - Guests group

                          Note: These are built-in security groups. "Local account" is more restrictive but may cause issues on servers such as systems that provide failover clustering.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254435
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-MS-000080 - Windows Server 2022 Deny access to this computer from the network user right on domain-joined member servers must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and local accounts and from unauthenticated access on all systems.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Deny access to this computer from the network" user right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on from the network.

                          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower-trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

                          Local accounts on domain-joined systems must also be assigned this right to decrease the risk of lateral movement resulting from credential theft attacks.

                          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                                  • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                                    • false (Deny access to this computer from the network - Local account)
                                    • false (Deny access to this computer from the network - Local account and member of Administrators group)
                                  • false (Deny access to this computer from the network - Domain Admins)
                                  • false (Deny access to this computer from the network - Enterprise Admins)
                                  • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                                    • true (The system is a Windows Domain Member Server)
                                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                                  • false (The system is a Windows Standalone Server)
                              • false (Deny access to this computer from the network - Guests)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349103 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Deny access to this computer from the network - Local account
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000007 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • security_principle must match the pattern '^(?i)(NT AUTHORITY\\)?Local account$'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349100 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Deny access to this computer from the network
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenynetworklogonright must be equal to '1'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '1' for:
                          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '0' for
                          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(NT AUTHORITY\\)?Local account$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25443500 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Deny access to this computer from the network - Local account and member of Administrators group
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25443500 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • security_principle must match the pattern '^(?i)(NT AUTHORITY\\)?Local account and member of Administrators group$'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349100 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Deny access to this computer from the network
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenynetworklogonright must be equal to '1'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '1' for:
                          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '0' for
                          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(NT AUTHORITY\\)?Local account and member of Administrators group$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349101 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Deny access to this computer from the network - Domain Admins
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000003 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • security_principle must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349100 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Deny access to this computer from the network
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenynetworklogonright must be equal to '1'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '1' for:
                          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '0' for
                          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349102 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Deny access to this computer from the network - Enterprise Admins
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000004 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • security_principle must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349100 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Deny access to this computer from the network
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenynetworklogonright must be equal to '1'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '1' for:
                          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '0' for
                          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:10000400 (wmi57_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:The system is a Windows Domain Member Server
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:10000100 (wmi57_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • namespace must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
                          • wql must be equal to 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:10000400 (wmi57_state)
                          State Comment:Domain Member Server
                          State Requirements:
                          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
                            • all domainrole must be equal to '3'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • namespace equals 'root\cimv2'
                          • wql equals 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'
                          • collected 'result' result:
                            • domainrole = '3'
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:10000300 (wmi57_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:The system is a Windows Standalone Server
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.

                          • namespace must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
                          • wql must be equal to 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'

                          • for all 'result' the following must be true:
                            • all domainrole must be equal to '2'
                          Collected Item/State Result:

                          • wql equals 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'
                          • collected 'result' result:
                            • domainrole = '3'

                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349100 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Deny access to this computer from the network - Guests
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.

                          • sedenynetworklogonright equals '0' for Guests
                          Result Analysis:sedenynetworklogonright : Guests = '0' failed test of 'equals' compared to '1'

                          V-254436 - Windows Server 2022 Deny log on as a batch job user right on domain-joined member servers must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and from unauthenticated access on all systems.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254436r958472_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_StandaloneServer
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-MS-000090
                          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Deny log on as a batch job" user right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on to the system as a batch job, such as Task Scheduler.

                          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower-trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

                          The Guests group must be assigned to prevent unauthenticated access.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Deny log on as a batch job to include the following:

                          Domain Systems Only:
                          - Enterprise Admins Group
                          - Domain Admins Group

                          All Systems:
                          - Guests Group
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254436
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-MS-000090 - Windows Server 2022 Deny log on as a batch job user right on domain-joined member servers must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and from unauthenticated access on all systems.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Deny log on as a batch job" user right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on to the system as a batch job, such as Task Scheduler.

                          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower-trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

                          The Guests group must be assigned to prevent unauthenticated access.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                                • false (Deny log on as a batch job is assigned to Guests)
                                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                                  • false (The system is a Windows Standalone Server)
                              • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                                • false (Deny log on as a batch job is assigned to Guests)
                                • false (The 'Deny log on as a batch job' user right is assigned to the Domain Admin groups.)
                                • false (The 'Deny log on as a batch job' user right is assigned to the Enterprise Admin groups.)
                                • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                                  • true (The system is a Windows Domain Member Server)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25442200 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Deny log on as a batch job is assigned to Guests
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000006 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • security_principle must be equal (case insensitive) to 'Guests'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349200 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenybatchLogonright must be equal to '1'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • sedenybatchLogonright equals '1' for:
                          • sedenybatchLogonright equals '0' for Guests
                          Result Analysis:sedenybatchLogonright : Guests = '0' failed test of 'equals' compared to '1'
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:10000300 (wmi57_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:The system is a Windows Standalone Server
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:10000100 (wmi57_object)
                          Object Comment:
