                          V-254437 - Windows Server 2022 Deny log on as a service user right on domain-joined member servers must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts. No other groups or accounts must be assigned this right.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254437r958472_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-MS-000100
                          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Deny log on as a service" user right defines accounts that are denied logon as a service.

                          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower-trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

                          Incorrect configurations could prevent services from starting and result in a denial of service.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Deny log on as a service to include the following:

                          Domain systems:
                          - Enterprise Admins Group
                          - Domain Admins Group
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254437
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-MS-000100 - Windows Server 2022 Deny log on as a service user right on domain-joined member servers must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts. No other groups or accounts must be assigned this right.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Deny log on as a service" user right defines accounts that are denied logon as a service.

                          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower-trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

                          Incorrect configurations could prevent services from starting and result in a denial of service.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Deny log on as a service - Domain Admins)
                              • false (Deny log on as a service - Enterprise Admins)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349300 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Deny log on as a service - Domain Admins
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000003 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • security_principle must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349300 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Deny log on as a service
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenyservicelogonright must be equal to '1'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • sedenyservicelogonright equals '1' for:
                          • sedenyservicelogonright equals '0' for
                          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349301 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Deny log on as a service - Enterprise Admins
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000004 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • security_principle must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349300 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Deny log on as a service
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenyservicelogonright must be equal to '1'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • sedenyservicelogonright equals '1' for:
                          • sedenyservicelogonright equals '0' for
                          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254438 - Windows Server 2022 Deny log on locally user right on domain-joined member servers must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and from unauthenticated access on all systems.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254438r958472_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_StandaloneServer
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-MS-000110
                          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Deny log on locally" user right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on interactively.

                          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower-trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

                          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Deny log on locally to include the following:

                          Domain Systems Only:
                          - Enterprise Admins Group
                          - Domain Admins Group

                          All Systems:
                          - Guests Group
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254438
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-MS-000110 - Windows Server 2022 Deny log on locally user right on domain-joined member servers must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and from unauthenticated access on all systems.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Deny log on locally" user right defines accounts that are prevented from logging on interactively.

                          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower-trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

                          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                                  • false (Deny log on locally - Domain Admins)
                                  • false (Deny log on as a locally - Enterprise Admins)
                                • false (System is not a member of a domain)
                              • false (Deny log on locally - Guests)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349402 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Deny log on locally - Domain Admins
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000003 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • security_principle must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349400 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Deny log on locally
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenyinteractivelogonright must be equal to '1'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • sedenyinteractivelogonright equals '1' for:
                          • sedenyinteractivelogonright equals '0' for
                          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349403 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Deny log on as a locally - Enterprise Admins
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000004 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • security_principle must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Enterprise Admins$'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349400 (accesstoken_state)

                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349401 (wmi57_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:System is not a member of a domain
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:10000100 (wmi57_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • namespace must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
                          • wql must be equal to 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:10000100 (wmi57_state)
                          State Comment:Standalone Workstation
                          State Requirements:

                            • all domainrole must be equal to '0'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:10000300 (wmi57_state)

                            • all domainrole must be equal to '2'

                          • wql equals 'SELECT DomainRole FROM win32_computersystem'
                          • collected 'result' result:
                            • domainrole = '3'
                          Result Analysis:domainrole = '3' failed test of 'equals' compared to '2'
                          domainrole = '3' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'

                          • sedenyinteractivelogonright equals '0' for Guests
                          Result Analysis:sedenyinteractivelogonright : Guests = '0' failed test of 'equals' compared to '1'

                          V-254439 - Windows Server 2022 Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services user right on domain-joined member servers must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and all local accounts and from unauthenticated access on all systems.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254439r958672_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_StandaloneServer
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-MS-000120
                          Identities:CCI-002314 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-17 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-17 (1))
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" user right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on using Remote Desktop Services.

                          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower-trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

                          Local accounts on domain-joined systems must also be assigned this right to decrease the risk of lateral movement resulting from credential theft attacks.

                          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services to include the following:

                          Domain Systems Only:
                          - Enterprise Admins group
                          - Domain Admins group
                          - Local account (see Note below)

                          All Systems:
                          - Guests group

                          Note: "Local account" is referring to the Windows built-in security group.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254439
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-MS-000120 - Windows Server 2022 Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services user right on domain-joined member servers must be configured to prevent access from highly privileged domain accounts and all local accounts and from unauthenticated access on all systems.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services" user right defines the accounts that are prevented from logging on using Remote Desktop Services.

                          In an Active Directory Domain, denying logons to the Enterprise Admins and Domain Admins groups on lower-trust systems helps mitigate the risk of privilege escalation from credential theft attacks, which could lead to the compromise of an entire domain.

                          Local accounts on domain-joined systems must also be assigned this right to decrease the risk of lateral movement resulting from credential theft attacks.

                          The Guests group must be assigned this right to prevent unauthenticated access.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                                • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                                  • false (Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Domain Admins)
                                  • false (Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Enterprise Admins)
                                  • false (Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Local account)
                                  • true (System is a Windows non-DC domain-joined system)
                                • false (The system is a Windows Standalone Workstation or Server)
                              • false (Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Guests)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349501 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Domain Admins
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:At least one collected item must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000003 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment:

                          • security_principle must match the pattern '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349500 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright must be equal to '1'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright equals '1' for:

                          Result Analysis:Security Principle Not Found: '^(?i)(.+\\)?Domain Admins$'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349500 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services - Guests
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000006 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • security_principle must be equal (case insensitive) to 'Guests'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349500 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Deny log on through Remote Desktop Services
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright must be equal to '1'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright equals '1' for:
                          • sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright equals '0' for Guests
                          Result Analysis:sedenyremoteInteractivelogonright : Guests = '0' failed test of 'equals' compared to '1'

                          V-254440 - Windows Server 2022 Enable computer and user accounts to be trusted for delegation user right must not be assigned to any groups or accounts on domain-joined member servers and standalone or nondomain-joined systems.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254440r958726_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_MemberServer
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_StandaloneServer
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-MS-000130
                          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Enable computer and user accounts to be trusted for delegation" user right allows the "Trusted for Delegation" setting to be changed. This could allow unauthorized users to impersonate other users.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Enable computer and user accounts to be trusted for delegation to be defined but containing no entries (blank).
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254440
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-MS-000130 - Windows Server 2022 Enable computer and user accounts to be trusted for delegation user right must not be assigned to any groups or accounts on domain-joined member servers and standalone or nondomain-joined systems.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Enable computer and user accounts to be trusted for delegation" user right allows the "Trusted for Delegation" setting to be changed. This could allow unauthorized users to impersonate other users.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true (Enable computer and user accounts to be trusted for delegation - None)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349600 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:Enable computer and user accounts to be trusted for delegation - None
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000008 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment:All security principles
                          Object Requirements:
                          • security_principle must match the pattern '.+'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349600 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Enable computer and user accounts to be trusted for delegation
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', seenabledelegationprivilege must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • seenabledelegationprivilege equals '1' for:
                          • seenabledelegationprivilege equals '0' for Access Control Assistance Operators, Administrators, Backup Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, Cryptographic Operators, Device Owners, Distributed COM Users, Event Log Readers, Guests, Hyper-V Administrators, IIS_IUSRS, Network Configuration Operators, OpenSSH Users, Performance Log Users, Performance Monitor Users, Power Users, Print Operators, RDS Endpoint Servers, RDS Management Servers, RDS Remote Access Servers, Remote Desktop Users, Remote Management Users, Replicator, Storage Replica Administrators, System Managed Accounts Group, User Mode Hardware Operators, Users, Administrator, DefaultAccount, Guest, WDAGUtilityAccount, NETWORK SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, SYSTEM, IUSR, TERMINAL SERVER USER, Authenticated Users, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, PROXY, ANONYMOUS LOGON, SERVICE, INTERACTIVE, BATCH, NETWORK, DIALUP, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, CREATOR GROUP, CREATOR OWNER, Everyone, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Window Manager\Window Manager Group

                          V-254442 - Windows Server 2022 must have the DoD Root Certificate Authority (CA) certificates installed in the Trusted Root Store.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254442r958448_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-PK-000010
                          Identities:CCI-000185 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (2); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (2).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (2) (a); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (2) (b) (1))
                          CCI-002470 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-23 (5); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-23 (5))
                          Description: To ensure secure DoD websites and DoD-signed code are properly validated, the system must trust the DoD Root CAs. The DoD root certificates will ensure that the trust chain is established for server certificates issued from the DoD CAs.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000066-GPOS-00034, SRG-OS-000403-GPOS-00182
                          Fix Text:Install the DoD Root CA certificates:

                          DoD Root CA 3
                          DoD Root CA 4
                          DoD Root CA 5
                          DoD Root CA 6

                          The InstallRoot tool is available on Cyber Exchange at https://cyber.mil/pki-pke/tools-configuration-files. Certificate bundles published by the PKI can be found at https://crl.gds.disa.mil/.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254442
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-PK-000010 - Windows Server 2022 must have the DoD Root Certificate Authority (CA) certificates installed in the Trusted Root Store.
                          Description:To ensure secure DoD websites and DoD-signed code are properly validated, the system must trust the DoD Root CAs. The DoD root certificates will ensure that the trust chain is established for server certificates issued from the DoD CAs.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000066-GPOS-00034, SRG-OS-000403-GPOS-00182
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (The DoD Root CA 3 Certificate expiring 12/30/2029 is installed into the Trusted Root Store.)
                              • false (The DoD Root CA 4 Certificate expiring 7/25/2032 is installed into the Trusted Root Store.)
                              • false (The DoD Root CA 5 Certificate expiring 6/14/2041 is installed into the Trusted Root Store.)
                              • false (The DoD Root CA 6 Certificate expiring 1/24/2053 is installed into the Trusted Root Store.)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25342700 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:The DoD Root CA 3 Certificate expiring 12/30/2029 is installed into the Trusted Root Store.
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:Result is based on check existence only.
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25342700 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Result Analysis:Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25342701 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:The DoD Root CA 4 Certificate expiring 7/25/2032 is installed into the Trusted Root Store.
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:Result is based on check existence only.
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25342705 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Result Analysis:Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25342702 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:The DoD Root CA 5 Certificate expiring 6/14/2041 is installed into the Trusted Root Store.
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:Result is based on check existence only.
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25342710 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Result Analysis:Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25342703 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:The DoD Root CA 6 Certificate expiring 1/24/2053 is installed into the Trusted Root Store.
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:Result is based on check existence only.
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25342715 (registry_object)

                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Result Analysis:Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254443 - Windows Server 2022 must have the DoD Interoperability Root Certificate Authority (CA) cross-certificates installed in the Untrusted Certificates Store on unclassified systems.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254443r958448_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-PK-000020
                          Identities:CCI-000185 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (2); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (2).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (2) (a); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (2) (b) (1))
                          CCI-002470 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-23 (5); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-23 (5))
                          Description: To ensure users do not experience denial of service when performing certificate-based authentication to DoD websites due to the system chaining to a root other than DoD Root CAs, the DoD Interoperability Root CA cross-certificates must be installed in the Untrusted Certificate Store. This requirement only applies to unclassified systems.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000066-GPOS-00034, SRG-OS-000403-GPOS-00182
                          Fix Text:Install the DoD Interoperability Root CA cross-certificates on unclassified systems.

                          Issued To - Issued By - Thumbprint

                          DoD Root CA 3- DoD Interoperability Root CA 2 - 49CBE933151872E17C8EAE7F0ABA97FB610F6477

                          Administrators must run the Federal Bridge Certification Authority (FBCA) Cross-Certificate Removal Tool once as an administrator and once as the current user.

                          The FBCA Cross-Certificate Remover Tool and User Guide are available on Cyber Exchange at https://cyber.mil/pki-pke/tools-configuration-files. Certificate bundles published by the PKI can be found at https://crl.gds.disa.mil/.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254443
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-PK-000020 - Windows Server 2022 must have the DoD Interoperability Root Certificate Authority (CA) cross-certificates installed in the Untrusted Certificates Store on unclassified systems.
                          Description:To ensure users do not experience denial of service when performing certificate-based authentication to DoD websites due to the system chaining to a root other than DoD Root CAs, the DoD Interoperability Root CA cross-certificates must be installed in the Untrusted Certificate Store. This requirement only applies to unclassified systems.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000066-GPOS-00034, SRG-OS-000403-GPOS-00182
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (DoD Interoperability Root CA 2 certificate expiring 11/16/2024 is installed in the Untrusted Certificates Store)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25342900 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:DoD Interoperability Root CA 2 certificate expiring 11/16/2024 is installed in the Untrusted Certificates Store
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:Result is based on check existence only.
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25342900 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Result Analysis:Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254444 - Windows Server 2022 must have the US DoD CCEB Interoperability Root CA cross-certificates in the Untrusted Certificates Store on unclassified systems.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254444r958448_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-PK-000030
                          Identities:CCI-000185 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (2); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (2).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (2) (a); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (2) (b) (1))
                          CCI-002470 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-23 (5); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-23 (5))
                          Description: To ensure users do not experience denial of service when performing certificate-based authentication to DoD websites due to the system chaining to a root other than DoD Root CAs, the US DoD CCEB Interoperability Root CA cross-certificates must be installed in the Untrusted Certificate Store. This requirement only applies to unclassified systems.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000066-GPOS-00034, SRG-OS-000403-GPOS-00182
                          Fix Text:Install the US DoD CCEB Interoperability Root CA cross-certificate on unclassified systems.

                          Issued To - Issued By - Thumbprint

                          DoD Root CA 3 - US DoD CCEB Interoperability Root CA 2 - 9B74964506C7ED9138070D08D5F8B969866560C8

                          Administrators must run the Federal Bridge Certification Authority (FBCA) Cross-Certificate Removal Tool once as an administrator and once as the current user.

                          The FBCA Cross-Certificate Remover Tool and User Guide are available on Cyber Exchange at https://cyber.mil/pki-pke/tools-configuration-files. PKI can be found at https://crl.gds.disa.mil/.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:

                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022

This requirement only applies to unclassified systems.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000066-GPOS-00034, SRG-OS-000403-GPOS-00182
                          Class:compliance

                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (US DoD CCEB Interoperability Root CA 2 cross-certificate expiring 7/18/2025 is installed in the Untrusted Certificates Store)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25343000 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:US DoD CCEB Interoperability Root CA 2 cross-certificate expiring 7/18/2025 is installed in the Untrusted Certificates Store
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:Result is based on check existence only.
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25343000 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment:

                          Result Analysis:Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254445 - Windows Server 2022 must have the built-in guest account disabled.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254445r958504_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-SO-000010
                          Identities:CCI-000804 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-8; NIST SP 800-53A: IA-8.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-8; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-8)
                          Description: A system faces an increased vulnerability threat if the built-in guest account is not disabled. This is a known account that exists on all Windows systems and cannot be deleted. This account is initialized during the installation of the operating system with no password assigned.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> Accounts: Guest account status to "Disabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254445
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-SO-000010 - Windows Server 2022 must have the built-in guest account disabled.
                          Description:A system faces an increased vulnerability threat if the built-in guest account is not disabled. This is a known account that exists on all Windows systems and cannot be deleted. This account is initialized during the installation of the operating system with no password assigned.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true (The built-in guest account is disabled.)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25343300 (user_sid55_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:The built-in guest account is disabled.
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25343300 (user_sid55_object)
                          Object Comment:Built-in Guest account
                          Object Requirements:
                          • for user_sid, the following must be true:
                            • user_sid must be equal to 'S-1-5-21-626057286-1122027226-1801680456-501'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000017 (user_sid55_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', enabled must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • user_sid equals 'S-1-5-21-626057286-1122027226-1801680456-501'
                          • enabled equals 'false'
                          • group_sid equals 'S-1-5-32-546'
                          • last_logon equals '0'

                          V-254447 - Windows Server 2022 built-in administrator account must be renamed.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254447r991589_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-SO-000030
                          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
                          Description: The built-in administrator account is a well-known account subject to attack. Renaming this account to an unidentified name improves the protection of this account and the system.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> Accounts: Rename administrator account to a name other than "Administrator".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254447
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-SO-000030 - Windows Server 2022 built-in administrator account must be renamed.
                          Description:The built-in administrator account is a well-known account subject to attack. Renaming this account to an unidentified name improves the protection of this account and the system.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Accounts: Rename administrator account' is not set to 'Administrator' or 'localdomain\Administrator')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25343500 (sid_sid_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Accounts: Rename administrator account' is not set to 'Administrator' or 'localdomain\Administrator'
                          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          State Operator:All item-state comparisons must be true.
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25343500 (sid_sid_object)
                          Object Comment:Built-in Administrator SID
                          Object Requirements:
                          • trustee_sid must match the pattern '^S-1-5-[0-9-]+-500$'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25343500 (sid_sid_state)
                          State Comment:Not Administrator
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', trustee_name must not be equal (case insensitive) to 'Administrator'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25343501 (sid_sid_state)
                          State Comment:Not localdomain\Administrator
                          State Requirements:
                          • for check = 'all', trustee_name, the following must be true:
                            • trustee_name must not be equal (case insensitive) to 'LAB-WORKSTATION01\Administrator'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • trustee_sid equals 'S-1-5-21-626057286-1122027226-1801680456-500'
                          • trustee_name equals 'Administrator'
                          • trustee_domain equals 'LAB-WORKSTATION01'
                          Result Analysis:'Administrator' failed test of 'case insensitive not equal' compared to 'Administrator'

                          V-254448 - Windows Server 2022 built-in guest account must be renamed.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254448r991589_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-SO-000040
                          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
                          Description: The built-in guest account is a well-known user account on all Windows systems and, as initially installed, does not require a password. This can allow access to system resources by unauthorized users. Renaming this account to an unidentified name improves the protection of this account and the system.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> Accounts: Rename guest account to a name other than "Guest".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254448
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-SO-000040 - Windows Server 2022 built-in guest account must be renamed.
                          Description:The built-in guest account is a well-known user account on all Windows systems and, as initially installed, does not require a password. This can allow access to system resources by unauthorized users. Renaming this account to an unidentified name improves the protection of this account and the system.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('Accounts: Rename Guest account' is not set to 'Guest' or 'localdomain\Guest')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25343600 (sid_sid_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'Accounts: Rename Guest account' is not set to 'Guest' or 'localdomain\Guest'
                          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          State Operator:All item-state comparisons must be true.
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25343600 (sid_sid_object)
                          Object Comment:Built-in Guest SID
                          Object Requirements:
                          • trustee_sid must match the pattern '^S-1-5-[0-9-]+-501$'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25343600 (sid_sid_state)
                          State Comment:Not Guest
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', trustee_name must not be equal (case insensitive) to 'Guest'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25343601 (sid_sid_state)
                          State Comment:Not localdomain\Guest
                          State Requirements:
                          • for check = 'all', trustee_name, the following must be true:
                            • trustee_name must not be equal (case insensitive) to 'LAB-WORKSTATION01\Guest'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • trustee_sid equals 'S-1-5-21-626057286-1122027226-1801680456-501'
                          • trustee_name equals 'Guest'
                          • trustee_domain equals 'LAB-WORKSTATION01'
                          Result Analysis:'Guest' failed test of 'case insensitive not equal' compared to 'Guest'

                          V-254449 - Windows Server 2022 must force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254449r958442_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-SO-000050
                          Identities:CCI-000169 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 a; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 a; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 a)
                          Description: Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. This setting allows administrators to enable more precise auditing capabilities.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> Audit: Force audit policy subcategory settings (Windows Vista or later) to override audit policy category settings to "Enabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254449
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-SO-000050 - Windows Server 2022 must force audit policy subcategory settings to override audit policy category settings.
                          Description:Maintaining an audit trail of system activity logs can help identify configuration errors, troubleshoot service disruptions, and analyze compromises that have occurred, as well as detect attacks. Audit logs are necessary to provide a trail of evidence in case the system or network is compromised. Collecting this data is essential for analyzing the security of information assets and detecting signs of suspicious and unexpected behavior. This setting allows administrators to enable more precise auditing capabilities.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Audit policy using subcategories is enabled.)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25343700 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Audit policy using subcategories is enabled.
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25343700 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa'
                          • name must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SCENoApplyLegacyAuditPolicy'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\SCENoApplyLegacyAuditPolicy'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254450 - Windows Server 2022 setting Domain member: Digitally encrypt or sign secure channel data (always) must be configured to Enabled.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254450r958908_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-SO-000060
                          Identities:CCI-002418 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-8; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-8)
                          CCI-002421 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-8 (1); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-8 (1))
                          Description: Requests sent on the secure channel are authenticated, and sensitive information (such as passwords) is encrypted, but not all information is encrypted. If this policy is enabled, outgoing secure channel traffic will be encrypted and signed.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000423-GPOS-00187, SRG-OS-000424-GPOS-00188
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> Domain member: Digitally encrypt or sign secure channel data (always) to "Enabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254450
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-SO-000060 - Windows Server 2022 setting Domain member: Digitally encrypt or sign secure channel data (always) must be configured to Enabled.
                          Description:Requests sent on the secure channel are authenticated, and sensitive information (such as passwords) is encrypted, but not all information is encrypted. If this policy is enabled, outgoing secure channel traffic will be encrypted and signed.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000423-GPOS-00187, SRG-OS-000424-GPOS-00188
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true ('Domain member: Digitally encrypt or sign secure channel data (always)' is set to 'Enabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25343800 (registry_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:'Domain member: Digitally encrypt or sign secure channel data (always)' is set to 'Enabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25343800 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netlogon\Parameters'
                          • name must be equal to 'RequireSignOrSeal'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netlogon\Parameters'
                          • name equals 'RequireSignOrSeal'
                          • last_write_time equals '134244551010000000'
                          • type equals 'reg_dword'
                          • value equals '1'
                          • windows_view equals '64_bit'
