                          V-254479 - Windows Server 2022 users must be required to enter a password to access private keys stored on the computer.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254479r958450_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-SO-000350
                          Identities:CCI-000186 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (2); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (2).1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (2) (b); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (2) (a) (1))
                          Description: If the private key is discovered, an attacker can use the key to authenticate as an authorized user and gain access to the network infrastructure.

                          The cornerstone of the PKI is the private key used to encrypt or digitally sign information.

                          If the private key is stolen, this will lead to the compromise of the authentication and nonrepudiation gained through PKI because the attacker can use the private key to digitally sign documents and pretend to be the authorized user.

                          Both the holders of a digital certificate and the issuing authority must protect the computers, storage devices, or whatever they use to keep the private keys.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> System cryptography: Force strong key protection for user keys stored on the computer to "User must enter a password each time they use a key".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254479
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-SO-000350 - Windows Server 2022 users must be required to enter a password to access private keys stored on the computer.
                          Description:If the private key is discovered, an attacker can use the key to authenticate as an authorized user and gain access to the network infrastructure.

                          The cornerstone of the PKI is the private key used to encrypt or digitally sign information.

                          If the private key is stolen, this will lead to the compromise of the authentication and nonrepudiation gained through PKI because the attacker can use the private key to digitally sign documents and pretend to be the authorized user.

                          Both the holders of a digital certificate and the issuing authority must protect the computers, storage devices, or whatever they use to keep the private keys.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Users are required to enter a password to access private keys stored on the computer.)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25447900 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Users are required to enter a password to access private keys stored on the computer.
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25447900 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Cryptography'
                          • name must be equal (case insensitive) to 'ForceKeyProtection'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000002 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '2'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Policies\Microsoft\Cryptography\ForceKeyProtection'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254480 - Windows Server 2022 must be configured to use FIPS-compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254480r1050599_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-SO-000360
                          Identities:CCI-002450 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-13; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-13 b)
                          Description: This setting ensures the system uses algorithms that are FIPS-compliant for encryption, hashing, and signing. FIPS-compliant algorithms meet specific standards established by the U.S. Government and must be the algorithms used for all OS encryption functions.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> System cryptography: Use FIPS compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing to "Enabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254480
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-SO-000360 - Windows Server 2022 must be configured to use FIPS-compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing.
                          Description:This setting ensures the system uses algorithms that are FIPS-compliant for encryption, hashing, and signing. FIPS-compliant algorithms meet specific standards established by the U.S. Government and must be the algorithms used for all OS encryption functions.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('System cryptography: Use FIPS compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing' is set to 'Enabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25346600 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'System cryptography: Use FIPS compliant algorithms for encryption, hashing, and signing' is set to 'Enabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25346600 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\FIPSAlgorithmPolicy'
                          • name must be equal to 'Enabled'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key equals 'SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\FIPSAlgorithmPolicy'
                          • name equals 'Enabled'
                          • last_write_time equals '133564285570000000'
                          • type equals 'reg_dword'
                          • value equals '0'
                          • windows_view equals '64_bit'
                          Result Analysis:HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\FIPSAlgorithmPolicy\Enabled\value = '0' failed test of 'equals' compared to '1'

                          V-254482 - Windows Server 2022 User Account Control (UAC) approval mode for the built-in Administrator must be enabled.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254482r1050789_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-SO-000380
                          Identities:CCI-004895 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-11 b)
                          CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11)
                          CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11)
                          Description: UAC is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. This setting configures the built-in Administrator account so that it runs in Admin Approval Mode.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00156, SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00157
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> User Account Control: Admin Approval Mode for the Built-in Administrator account to "Enabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254482
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-SO-000380 - Windows Server 2022 User Account Control (UAC) approval mode for the built-in Administrator must be enabled.
                          Description:UAC is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. This setting configures the built-in Administrator account so that it runs in Admin Approval Mode.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00156, SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00157
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Verifies 'User Account Control: Admin Approval Mode for the Built-in Administrator account' is set to 'Enabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25346800 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Verifies 'User Account Control: Admin Approval Mode for the Built-in Administrator account' is set to 'Enabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25346800 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
                          • name must be equal to 'FilterAdministratorToken'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Result Analysis:Registry Name Not found: 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\FilterAdministratorToken'. Existence requirement is 'at_least_one_exists' and no items were collected, so result is false.

                          V-254483 - Windows Server 2022 UIAccess applications must not be allowed to prompt for elevation without using the secure desktop.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254483r958518_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_NotServerCore
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-SO-000390
                          Identities:CCI-001084 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-3.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-3)
                          Description: User Account Control (UAC) is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. This setting prevents User Interface Accessibility programs from disabling the secure desktop for elevation prompts.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> User Account Control: Allow UIAccess applications to prompt for elevation without using the secure desktop to "Disabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254483
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-SO-000390 - Windows Server 2022 UIAccess applications must not be allowed to prompt for elevation without using the secure desktop.
                          Description:User Account Control (UAC) is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. This setting prevents User Interface Accessibility programs from disabling the secure desktop for elevation prompts.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true (UIAccess applications is not allowed to prompt for elevation without using the secure desktop.)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25448300 (registry_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:UIAccess applications is not allowed to prompt for elevation without using the secure desktop.
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25448300 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal (case insensitive) to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal (case insensitive) to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
                          • name must be equal (case insensitive) to 'EnableUIADesktopToggle'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
                          • name equals 'EnableUIADesktopToggle'
                          • last_write_time equals '134244553680000000'
                          • type equals 'reg_dword'
                          • value equals '0'
                          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

                          V-254484 - Windows Server 2022 User Account Control (UAC) must, at a minimum, prompt administrators for consent on the secure desktop.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254484r958518_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Applicability:Windows_NotServerCore
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-SO-000400
                          Identities:CCI-001084 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-3.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-3)
                          Description: UAC is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. This setting configures the elevation requirements for logged-on administrators to complete a task that requires raised privileges.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> User Account Control: Behavior of the elevation prompt for administrators in Admin Approval Mode to "Prompt for consent on the secure desktop".

                          The more secure option for this setting, "Prompt for credentials on the secure desktop", would also be acceptable.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254484
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-SO-000400 - Windows Server 2022 User Account Control (UAC) must, at a minimum, prompt administrators for consent on the secure desktop.
                          Description:UAC is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. This setting configures the elevation requirements for logged-on administrators to complete a task that requires raised privileges.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false ('User Account Control: Behavior of the elevation prompt for administrators in Admin Approval Mode' is set to 'Prompt for consent on the secure desktop' or 'Prompt for credentials on the secure desktop')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25448400 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:'User Account Control: Behavior of the elevation prompt for administrators in Admin Approval Mode' is set to 'Prompt for consent on the secure desktop' or 'Prompt for credentials on the secure desktop'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          State Operator:One or more item-state comparisons may be true.
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25346900 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
                          • name must be equal to 'ConsentPromptBehaviorAdmin'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000002 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '2'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
                          • name equals 'ConsentPromptBehaviorAdmin'
                          • last_write_time equals '134244553680000000'
                          • type equals 'reg_dword'
                          • value equals '5'
                          • windows_view equals '64_bit'
                          Result Analysis:HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\ConsentPromptBehaviorAdmin\value = '5' failed test of 'equals' compared to '2'
                          HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\ConsentPromptBehaviorAdmin\value = '5' failed test of 'equals' compared to '1'

                          V-254485 - Windows Server 2022 User Account Control (UAC) must automatically deny standard user requests for elevation.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254485r1050789_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-SO-000410
                          Identities:CCI-004895 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-11 b)
                          CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11)
                          CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11)
                          Description: UAC is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. This setting controls the behavior of elevation when requested by a standard user account.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00156, SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00157
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> User Account Control: Behavior of the elevation prompt for standard users to "Automatically deny elevation requests".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254485
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-SO-000410 - Windows Server 2022 User Account Control (UAC) must automatically deny standard user requests for elevation.
                          Description:UAC is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. This setting controls the behavior of elevation when requested by a standard user account.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00156, SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00157
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Verifies 'User Account Control: Behavior of the elevation prompt for standard users' is set to 'Automatically deny elevation requests')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25347100 (registry_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Verifies 'User Account Control: Behavior of the elevation prompt for standard users' is set to 'Automatically deny elevation requests'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25347100 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
                          • name must be equal to 'ConsentPromptBehaviorUser'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000000 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key equals 'Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
                          • name equals 'ConsentPromptBehaviorUser'
                          • last_write_time equals '134244553680000000'
                          • type equals 'reg_dword'
                          • value equals '3'
                          • windows_view equals '64_bit'
                          Result Analysis:HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\ConsentPromptBehaviorUser\value = '3' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'

                          V-254486 - Windows Server 2022 User Account Control (UAC) must be configured to detect application installations and prompt for elevation.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254486r958518_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-SO-000420
                          Identities:CCI-001084 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-3.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-3)
                          Description: UAC is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. This setting requires Windows to respond to application installation requests by prompting for credentials.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> User Account Control: Detect application installations and prompt for elevation to "Enabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254486
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-SO-000420 - Windows Server 2022 User Account Control (UAC) must be configured to detect application installations and prompt for elevation.
                          Description:UAC is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. This setting requires Windows to respond to application installation requests by prompting for credentials.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true ('User Account Control: Detect application installations and prompt for elevation' is set to 'Enabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25347200 (registry_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:'User Account Control: Detect application installations and prompt for elevation' is set to 'Enabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25347200 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
                          • name must be equal to 'EnableInstallerDetection'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
                          • name equals 'EnableInstallerDetection'
                          • last_write_time equals '134244553680000000'
                          • type equals 'reg_dword'
                          • value equals '1'
                          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

                          V-254487 - Windows Server 2022 User Account Control (UAC) must only elevate UIAccess applications that are installed in secure locations.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254487r958518_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-SO-000430
                          Identities:CCI-001084 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-3.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-3)
                          Description: UAC is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. This setting configures Windows to only allow applications installed in a secure location on the file system, such as the Program Files or the Windows\System32 folders, to run with elevated privileges.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> User Account Control: Only elevate UIAccess applications that are installed in secure locations to "Enabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254487
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-SO-000430 - Windows Server 2022 User Account Control (UAC) must only elevate UIAccess applications that are installed in secure locations.
                          Description:UAC is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. This setting configures Windows to only allow applications installed in a secure location on the file system, such as the Program Files or the Windows\System32 folders, to run with elevated privileges.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true ('User Account Control: Only elevate UIAccess applications that are installed in secure locations' is set to 'Enabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25347300 (registry_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:'User Account Control: Only elevate UIAccess applications that are installed in secure locations' is set to 'Enabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25347300 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
                          • name must be equal to 'EnableSecureUIAPaths'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key equals 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
                          • name equals 'EnableSecureUIAPaths'
                          • last_write_time equals '134244553680000000'
                          • type equals 'reg_dword'
                          • value equals '1'
                          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

                          V-254488 - Windows Server 2022 User Account Control (UAC) must run all administrators in Admin Approval Mode, enabling UAC.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254488r1050789_rule
                          Test Type:Automated

                          CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11)
                          CCI-002038 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-11)
                          Description: UAC is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. This setting enables UAC.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00156, SRG-OS-000373-GPOS-00157
