                          V-254489 - Windows Server 2022 User Account Control (UAC) must virtualize file and registry write failures to per-user locations.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254489r958518_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-SO-000450
                          Identities:CCI-001084 (NIST SP 800-53: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: SC-3.1 (ii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SC-3)
                          Description: UAC is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. This setting configures non-UAC-compliant applications to run in virtualized file and registry entries in per-user locations, allowing them to run.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> Security Options >> User Account Control: Virtualize file and registry write failures to per-user locations to "Enabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254489
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-SO-000450 - Windows Server 2022 User Account Control (UAC) must virtualize file and registry write failures to per-user locations.
                          Description:UAC is a security mechanism for limiting the elevation of privileges, including administrative accounts, unless authorized. This setting configures non-UAC-compliant applications to run in virtualized file and registry entries in per-user locations, allowing them to run.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true ('User Account Control: Virtualize file and registry write failures to per-user locations' is set to 'Enabled')
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25347500 (registry_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:'User Account Control: Virtualize file and registry write failures to per-user locations' is set to 'Enabled'
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25347500 (registry_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • hive must be equal to 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key must be equal to 'Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
                          • name must be equal to 'EnableVirtualization'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:20000001 (registry_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', type must be equal to 'reg_dword'
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '1'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • hive equals 'HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE'
                          • key equals 'Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System'
                          • name equals 'EnableVirtualization'
                          • last_write_time equals '134244553680000000'
                          • type equals 'reg_dword'
                          • value equals '1'
                          • windows_view equals '64_bit'

                          V-254490 - Windows Server 2022 must preserve zone information when saving attachments.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254490r991589_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-UC-000010
                          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
                          Description: Attachments from outside sources may contain malicious code. Preserving zone of origin (internet, intranet, local, restricted) information on file attachments allows Windows to determine risk.
                          Fix Text:The default behavior is for Windows to mark file attachments with their zone information.

                          If this needs to be corrected, configure the policy value for User Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> Windows Components >> Attachment Manager >> Do not preserve zone information in file attachments to "Not Configured" or "Disabled".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:def:5041
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:SRG-OS-000480-GPOS-00227, SV-254490r991589
                          Description:Windows Server 2022 must preserve zone information when saving attachments.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (Zone information must be preserved when saving attachments. SaveZoneInformation must be set to 2 if value exists.)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:tst:5041 (ntuser_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:Zone information must be preserved when saving attachments. SaveZoneInformation must be set to 2 if value exists.
                          Check Existence:Zero or more collected items may exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:obj:5041 (ntuser_object)
                          Object Comment:Windows Explorer NoPreviewPane Setting
                          Object Requirements:
                          • key must be equal to 'SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\Attachments'
                          • name must be equal to 'SaveZoneInformation'
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • enabled equals 'false'
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • for check = 'all', type, at least one of the following must be true:
                            • type equals ''
                          State ID:oval:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:ste:5041 (ntuser_state)
                          State Comment:Registry key type equals reg_dword and value equals 2
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', value must be equal to '2'

                          V-254491 - Windows Server 2022 Access Credential Manager as a trusted caller user right must not be assigned to any groups or accounts.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254491r958726_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-UR-000010
                          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Access Credential Manager as a trusted caller" user right may be able to retrieve the credentials of other accounts from Credential Manager.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Access Credential Manager as a trusted caller to be defined but containing no entries (blank).
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254491
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-UR-000010 - Windows Server 2022 Access Credential Manager as a trusted caller user right must not be assigned to any groups or accounts.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Access Credential Manager as a trusted caller" user right may be able to retrieve the credentials of other accounts from Credential Manager.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true (Access Credential Manager as a trusted caller - None)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25347900 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:Access Credential Manager as a trusted caller - None
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000008 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment:All security principles
                          Object Requirements:
                          • security_principle must match the pattern '.+'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25347900 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Access Credential Manager as a trusted caller
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', setrustedcredmanaccessnameright must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • setrustedcredmanaccessnameright equals '1' for:
                          • setrustedcredmanaccessnameright equals '0' for Access Control Assistance Operators, Administrators, Backup Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, Cryptographic Operators, Device Owners, Distributed COM Users, Event Log Readers, Guests, Hyper-V Administrators, IIS_IUSRS, Network Configuration Operators, OpenSSH Users, Performance Log Users, Performance Monitor Users, Power Users, Print Operators, RDS Endpoint Servers, RDS Management Servers, RDS Remote Access Servers, Remote Desktop Users, Remote Management Users, Replicator, Storage Replica Administrators, System Managed Accounts Group, User Mode Hardware Operators, Users, Administrator, DefaultAccount, Guest, WDAGUtilityAccount, NETWORK SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, SYSTEM, IUSR, TERMINAL SERVER USER, Authenticated Users, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, PROXY, ANONYMOUS LOGON, SERVICE, INTERACTIVE, BATCH, NETWORK, DIALUP, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, CREATOR GROUP, CREATOR OWNER, Everyone, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Window Manager\Window Manager Group

                          V-254493 - Windows Server 2022 Allow log on locally user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254493r958472_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-UR-000030
                          Identities:CCI-000213 (NIST SP 800-53: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53A: AC-3.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-3; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-3)
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Allow log on locally" user right can log on interactively to a system.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Allow log on locally to include only the following accounts or groups:

                          - Administrators
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254493
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-UR-000030 - Windows Server 2022 Allow log on locally user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Allow log on locally" user right can log on interactively to a system.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Allow log on locally - Administrators)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25449300 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Allow log on locally - Administrators
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000009 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment:Administrators
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25348200 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Allow log on locally
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', seinteractivelogonright must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • seinteractivelogonright equals '1' for: Users, Backup Operators
                          • seinteractivelogonright equals '0' for NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Authenticated Users, RDS Endpoint Servers, Guests, Performance Log Users, Guest, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, IUSR, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, Remote Management Users, TERMINAL SERVER USER, DIALUP, ANONYMOUS LOGON, DefaultAccount, Remote Desktop Users, Distributed COM Users, SYSTEM, NETWORK, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, PROXY, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, Replicator, Access Control Assistance Operators, Hyper-V Administrators, Performance Monitor Users, Storage Replica Administrators, Everyone, WDAGUtilityAccount, RDS Management Servers, Event Log Readers, SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, Network Configuration Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Administrator, Device Owners, Cryptographic Operators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, Print Operators, IIS_IUSRS, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, System Managed Accounts Group, INTERACTIVE, CREATOR OWNER, RDS Remote Access Servers, OpenSSH Users, User Mode Hardware Operators, NETWORK SERVICE
                          Result Analysis:seinteractivelogonright : Users = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'
                          seinteractivelogonright : Backup Operators = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'

                          V-254494 - Windows Server 2022 back up files and directories user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254494r958726_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-UR-000040
                          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Back up files and directories" user right can circumvent file and directory permissions and could allow access to sensitive data.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Back up files and directories to include only the following accounts or groups:

                          - Administrators
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254494
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-UR-000040 - Windows Server 2022 back up files and directories user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Back up files and directories" user right can circumvent file and directory permissions and could allow access to sensitive data.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Back up files and directories - Administrators)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25348300 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Back up files and directories - Administrators
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000009 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment:Administrators
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25348300 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Back up files and directories
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sebackupprivilege must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • sebackupprivilege equals '1' for: Backup Operators
                          • sebackupprivilege equals '0' for NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Authenticated Users, RDS Endpoint Servers, Guests, Users, Performance Log Users, Guest, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, IUSR, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, Remote Management Users, TERMINAL SERVER USER, DIALUP, ANONYMOUS LOGON, DefaultAccount, Remote Desktop Users, Distributed COM Users, SYSTEM, NETWORK, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, PROXY, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, Replicator, Access Control Assistance Operators, Hyper-V Administrators, Performance Monitor Users, Storage Replica Administrators, Everyone, WDAGUtilityAccount, RDS Management Servers, Event Log Readers, SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, Network Configuration Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Administrator, Device Owners, Cryptographic Operators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, Print Operators, IIS_IUSRS, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, System Managed Accounts Group, INTERACTIVE, CREATOR OWNER, RDS Remote Access Servers, OpenSSH Users, User Mode Hardware Operators, NETWORK SERVICE
                          Result Analysis:sebackupprivilege : Backup Operators = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'

                          V-254495 - Windows Server 2022 create a pagefile user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254495r958726_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-UR-000050
                          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Create a pagefile" user right can change the size of a pagefile, which could affect system performance.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Create a pagefile to include only the following accounts or groups:

                          - Administrators
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254495
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-UR-000050 - Windows Server 2022 create a pagefile user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Create a pagefile" user right can change the size of a pagefile, which could affect system performance.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true (Create a pagefile - Administrators)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25348500 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:Create a pagefile - Administrators
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000009 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment:Administrators
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25348500 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Create a pagefile
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', secreatepagefileprivilege must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • secreatepagefileprivilege equals '1' for:
                          • secreatepagefileprivilege equals '0' for NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Authenticated Users, RDS Endpoint Servers, Guests, Users, Performance Log Users, Guest, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, IUSR, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, Remote Management Users, TERMINAL SERVER USER, DIALUP, ANONYMOUS LOGON, DefaultAccount, Remote Desktop Users, Distributed COM Users, SYSTEM, NETWORK, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, PROXY, Backup Operators, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, Replicator, Access Control Assistance Operators, Hyper-V Administrators, Performance Monitor Users, Storage Replica Administrators, Everyone, WDAGUtilityAccount, RDS Management Servers, Event Log Readers, SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, Network Configuration Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Administrator, Device Owners, Cryptographic Operators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, Print Operators, IIS_IUSRS, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, System Managed Accounts Group, INTERACTIVE, CREATOR OWNER, RDS Remote Access Servers, OpenSSH Users, User Mode Hardware Operators, NETWORK SERVICE

                          V-254497 - Windows Server 2022 create global objects user right must only be assigned to Administrators, Service, Local Service, and Network Service.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254497r958726_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-UR-000070
                          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Create global objects" user right can create objects that are available to all sessions, which could affect processes in other users' sessions.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Create global objects to include only the following accounts or groups:

                          - Administrators
                          - Service
                          - Local Service
                          - Network Service
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254497
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-UR-000070 - Windows Server 2022 create global objects user right must only be assigned to Administrators, Service, Local Service, and Network Service.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Create global objects" user right can create objects that are available to all sessions, which could affect processes in other users' sessions.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true (Create global objects - Administrators, Service, Local Service, Network Service)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25348700 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:Create global objects - Administrators, Service, Local Service, Network Service
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000013 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment:Administrators, Local Service, Network Service, Service
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'LOCAL SERVICE'
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'NETWORK SERVICE'
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'SERVICE'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25348700 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Create global objects
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', secreateglobalprivilege must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • secreateglobalprivilege equals '1' for:
                          • secreateglobalprivilege equals '0' for NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Authenticated Users, RDS Endpoint Servers, Guests, Users, Performance Log Users, Guest, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, IUSR, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, Remote Management Users, TERMINAL SERVER USER, DIALUP, ANONYMOUS LOGON, DefaultAccount, Remote Desktop Users, Distributed COM Users, SYSTEM, NETWORK, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, PROXY, Backup Operators, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, Replicator, Access Control Assistance Operators, Hyper-V Administrators, Performance Monitor Users, Storage Replica Administrators, Everyone, WDAGUtilityAccount, RDS Management Servers, Event Log Readers, Network Configuration Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Administrator, Device Owners, Cryptographic Operators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, Print Operators, IIS_IUSRS, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, System Managed Accounts Group, INTERACTIVE, CREATOR OWNER, RDS Remote Access Servers, OpenSSH Users, User Mode Hardware Operators

                          V-254498 - Windows Server 2022 create permanent shared objects user right must not be assigned to any groups or accounts.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254498r958726_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-UR-000080
                          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Create permanent shared objects" user right could expose sensitive data by creating shared objects.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Create permanent shared objects to be defined but containing no entries (blank).
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254498
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-UR-000080 - Windows Server 2022 create permanent shared objects user right must not be assigned to any groups or accounts.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Create permanent shared objects" user right could expose sensitive data by creating shared objects.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true (No groups or accounts are granted the 'Create permanent shared objects' user right.)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25348800 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:No groups or accounts are granted the 'Create permanent shared objects' user right.
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000008 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment:All security principles
                          Object Requirements:
                          • security_principle must match the pattern '.+'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25348800 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', secreatepermanentprivilege must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • secreatepermanentprivilege equals '1' for:
                          • secreatepermanentprivilege equals '0' for Access Control Assistance Operators, Administrators, Backup Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, Cryptographic Operators, Device Owners, Distributed COM Users, Event Log Readers, Guests, Hyper-V Administrators, IIS_IUSRS, Network Configuration Operators, OpenSSH Users, Performance Log Users, Performance Monitor Users, Power Users, Print Operators, RDS Endpoint Servers, RDS Management Servers, RDS Remote Access Servers, Remote Desktop Users, Remote Management Users, Replicator, Storage Replica Administrators, System Managed Accounts Group, User Mode Hardware Operators, Users, Administrator, DefaultAccount, Guest, WDAGUtilityAccount, NETWORK SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, SYSTEM, IUSR, TERMINAL SERVER USER, Authenticated Users, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, PROXY, ANONYMOUS LOGON, SERVICE, INTERACTIVE, BATCH, NETWORK, DIALUP, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, CREATOR GROUP, CREATOR OWNER, Everyone, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Window Manager\Window Manager Group

                          V-254499 - Windows Server 2022 create symbolic links user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254499r958726_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-UR-000090
                          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Create symbolic links" user right can create pointers to other objects, which could expose the system to attack.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Create symbolic links to include only the following accounts or groups:

                          - Administrators

                          Systems that have the Hyper-V role will also have "Virtual Machines" given this user right. If this needs to be added manually, enter it as "NT Virtual Machine\Virtual Machines".
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254499
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-UR-000090 - Windows Server 2022 create symbolic links user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Create symbolic links" user right can create pointers to other objects, which could expose the system to attack.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (One or more child tests must be true. (OR))
                              • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                                • true (Create symbolic links - Administrators, Virtual Machines)
                                • true (Hyper-V Role is installed)
                              • true (Create symbolic links - Administrators)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25449901 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:Create symbolic links - Administrators, Virtual Machines
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25449901 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment:Filter for Administrators, Virtual Machines
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle matches the pattern '^(?i)(NT VIRTUAL MACHINE\\)?Virtual Machines$'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25348900 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Create symbolic links
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', secreatesymboliclinkprivilege must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • secreatesymboliclinkprivilege equals '1' for:
                          • secreatesymboliclinkprivilege equals '0' for NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Authenticated Users, RDS Endpoint Servers, Guests, Users, Performance Log Users, Guest, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, IUSR, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, Remote Management Users, TERMINAL SERVER USER, DIALUP, ANONYMOUS LOGON, DefaultAccount, Remote Desktop Users, Distributed COM Users, SYSTEM, NETWORK, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, PROXY, Backup Operators, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, Replicator, Access Control Assistance Operators, Hyper-V Administrators, Performance Monitor Users, Storage Replica Administrators, Everyone, WDAGUtilityAccount, RDS Management Servers, Event Log Readers, SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, Network Configuration Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Administrator, Device Owners, Cryptographic Operators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, Print Operators, IIS_IUSRS, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, System Managed Accounts Group, INTERACTIVE, CREATOR OWNER, RDS Remote Access Servers, OpenSSH Users, User Mode Hardware Operators, NETWORK SERVICE
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25449902 (wmi57_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:Hyper-V Role is installed
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:Result is based on check existence only.
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000014 (wmi57_object)
                          Object Comment: 
                          Object Requirements:
                          • namespace must be equal to 'root\cimv2'
                          • wql must be equal to 'SELECT ID FROM Win32_ServerFeature'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ exists ]
                          • namespace equals 'root\cimv2'
                          • wql equals 'SELECT ID FROM Win32_ServerFeature'
                          • collected 'result' result:
                            • id = '481'
                          • collected 'result' result:
                            • id = '418'
                          • collected 'result' result:
                            • id = '466'
                          • collected 'result' result:
                            • id = '420'
                          • collected 'result' result:
                            • id = '425'
                          • collected 'result' result:
                            • id = '412'

                            • id = '417'
                          • collected 'result' result:
                            • id = '482'

                            • id = '1043'

                          Result:true
                          Title:Create symbolic links - Administrators
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
