                          Object Comment:Administrators
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25348900 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Create symbolic links
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', secreatesymboliclinkprivilege must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • secreatesymboliclinkprivilege equals '1' for:
                          • secreatesymboliclinkprivilege equals '0' for NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Authenticated Users, RDS Endpoint Servers, Guests, Users, Performance Log Users, Guest, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, IUSR, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, Remote Management Users, TERMINAL SERVER USER, DIALUP, ANONYMOUS LOGON, DefaultAccount, Remote Desktop Users, Distributed COM Users, SYSTEM, NETWORK, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, PROXY, Backup Operators, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, Replicator, Access Control Assistance Operators, Hyper-V Administrators, Performance Monitor Users, Storage Replica Administrators, Everyone, WDAGUtilityAccount, RDS Management Servers, Event Log Readers, SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, Network Configuration Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Administrator, Device Owners, Cryptographic Operators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, Print Operators, IIS_IUSRS, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, System Managed Accounts Group, INTERACTIVE, CREATOR OWNER, RDS Remote Access Servers, OpenSSH Users, User Mode Hardware Operators, NETWORK SERVICE

                          V-254501 - Windows Server 2022 force shutdown from a remote system user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254501r958726_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-UR-000110
                          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Force shutdown from a remote system" user right can remotely shut down a system, which could result in a denial of service.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Force shutdown from a remote system to include only the following accounts or groups:

                          - Administrators
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254501
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-UR-000110 - Windows Server 2022 force shutdown from a remote system user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Force shutdown from a remote system" user right can remotely shut down a system, which could result in a denial of service.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true (Force shutdown from a remote system - Administrators)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349700 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:Force shutdown from a remote system - Administrators
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000009 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment:Administrators
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349700 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Force shutdown from a remote system
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', seremoteshutdownprivilege must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • seremoteshutdownprivilege equals '1' for:
                          • seremoteshutdownprivilege equals '0' for NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Authenticated Users, RDS Endpoint Servers, Guests, Users, Performance Log Users, Guest, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, IUSR, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, Remote Management Users, TERMINAL SERVER USER, DIALUP, ANONYMOUS LOGON, DefaultAccount, Remote Desktop Users, Distributed COM Users, SYSTEM, NETWORK, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, PROXY, Backup Operators, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, Replicator, Access Control Assistance Operators, Hyper-V Administrators, Performance Monitor Users, Storage Replica Administrators, Everyone, WDAGUtilityAccount, RDS Management Servers, Event Log Readers, SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, Network Configuration Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Administrator, Device Owners, Cryptographic Operators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, Print Operators, IIS_IUSRS, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, System Managed Accounts Group, INTERACTIVE, CREATOR OWNER, RDS Remote Access Servers, OpenSSH Users, User Mode Hardware Operators, NETWORK SERVICE

                          V-254502 - Windows Server 2022 generate security audits user right must only be assigned to Local Service and Network Service.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254502r958726_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-UR-000120
                          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Generate security audits" user right specifies users and processes that can generate Security Log audit records, which must only be the system service accounts defined.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Generate security audits to include only the following accounts or groups:

                          - Local Service
                          - Network Service
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254502
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-UR-000120 - Windows Server 2022 generate security audits user right must only be assigned to Local Service and Network Service.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Generate security audits" user right specifies users and processes that can generate Security Log audit records, which must only be the system service accounts defined.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true (Generate security audits - Local Service, Network Service)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25450200 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:Generate security audits - Local Service, Network Service
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:25450200 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment:Filter for Local Service and Network Service
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'LOCAL SERVICE'
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'NETWORK SERVICE'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25450200 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Generate security audits
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', seauditprivilege must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • seauditprivilege equals '1' for:
                          • seauditprivilege equals '0' for Administrators, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Authenticated Users, RDS Endpoint Servers, Guests, Users, Performance Log Users, Guest, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, IUSR, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, Remote Management Users, TERMINAL SERVER USER, DIALUP, ANONYMOUS LOGON, DefaultAccount, Remote Desktop Users, Distributed COM Users, SYSTEM, NETWORK, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, PROXY, Backup Operators, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, Replicator, Access Control Assistance Operators, Hyper-V Administrators, Performance Monitor Users, Storage Replica Administrators, Everyone, WDAGUtilityAccount, RDS Management Servers, Event Log Readers, SERVICE, Network Configuration Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Administrator, Device Owners, Cryptographic Operators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, Print Operators, IIS_IUSRS, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, System Managed Accounts Group, INTERACTIVE, CREATOR OWNER, RDS Remote Access Servers, OpenSSH Users, User Mode Hardware Operators

                          V-254503 - Windows Server 2022 impersonate a client after authentication user right must only be assigned to Administrators, Service, Local Service, and Network Service.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254503r958726_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-UR-000130
                          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Impersonate a client after authentication" user right allows a program to impersonate another user or account to run on their behalf. An attacker could use this to elevate privileges.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Impersonate a client after authentication to include only the following accounts or groups:

                          - Administrators
                          - Service
                          - Local Service
                          - Network Service
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254503
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-UR-000130 - Windows Server 2022 impersonate a client after authentication user right must only be assigned to Administrators, Service, Local Service, and Network Service.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Impersonate a client after authentication" user right allows a program to impersonate another user or account to run on their behalf. An attacker could use this to elevate privileges.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true (No groups or accounts are granted the 'Impersonate a client after authentication' user right.)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349800 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:No groups or accounts are granted the 'Impersonate a client after authentication' user right.
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000013 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment:Administrators, Local Service, Network Service, Service
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'LOCAL SERVICE'
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'NETWORK SERVICE'
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'SERVICE'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349800 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', seimpersonateprivilege must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • seimpersonateprivilege equals '1' for:
                          • seimpersonateprivilege equals '0' for NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Authenticated Users, RDS Endpoint Servers, Guests, Users, Performance Log Users, Guest, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, IUSR, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, Remote Management Users, TERMINAL SERVER USER, DIALUP, ANONYMOUS LOGON, DefaultAccount, Remote Desktop Users, Distributed COM Users, SYSTEM, NETWORK, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, PROXY, Backup Operators, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, Replicator, Access Control Assistance Operators, Hyper-V Administrators, Performance Monitor Users, Storage Replica Administrators, Everyone, WDAGUtilityAccount, RDS Management Servers, Event Log Readers, Network Configuration Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Administrator, Device Owners, Cryptographic Operators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, Print Operators, IIS_IUSRS, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, System Managed Accounts Group, INTERACTIVE, CREATOR OWNER, RDS Remote Access Servers, OpenSSH Users, User Mode Hardware Operators

                          V-254504 - Windows Server 2022 increase scheduling priority: user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254504r958726_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-UR-000140
                          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Increase scheduling priority" user right can change a scheduling priority, causing performance issues or a denial of service.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Increase scheduling priority to include only the following accounts or groups:

                          - Administrators
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254504
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-UR-000140 - Windows Server 2022 increase scheduling priority: user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Increase scheduling priority" user right can change a scheduling priority, causing performance issues or a denial of service.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Increase scheduling priority - Administrators)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25450400 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Increase scheduling priority - Administrators
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000009 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment:Administrators
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25450400 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', seincreasebasepriorityprivilege must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • seincreasebasepriorityprivilege equals '1' for: Window Manager\Window Manager Group
                          • seincreasebasepriorityprivilege equals '0' for NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Authenticated Users, RDS Endpoint Servers, Guests, Users, Performance Log Users, Guest, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, IUSR, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, Remote Management Users, TERMINAL SERVER USER, DIALUP, ANONYMOUS LOGON, DefaultAccount, Remote Desktop Users, Distributed COM Users, SYSTEM, NETWORK, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, PROXY, Backup Operators, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, Replicator, Access Control Assistance Operators, Hyper-V Administrators, Performance Monitor Users, Storage Replica Administrators, Everyone, WDAGUtilityAccount, RDS Management Servers, Event Log Readers, SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, Network Configuration Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Administrator, Device Owners, Cryptographic Operators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, Print Operators, IIS_IUSRS, System Managed Accounts Group, INTERACTIVE, CREATOR OWNER, RDS Remote Access Servers, OpenSSH Users, User Mode Hardware Operators, NETWORK SERVICE
                          Result Analysis:seincreasebasepriorityprivilege : Window Manager\Window Manager Group = '1' failed test of 'equals' compared to '0'

                          V-254505 - Windows Server 2022 load and unload device drivers user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254505r958726_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-UR-000150
                          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Load and unload device drivers" user right allows a user to load device drivers dynamically on a system. This could be used by an attacker to install malicious code.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Load and unload device drivers to include only the following accounts or groups:

                          - Administrators
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254505
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-UR-000150 - Windows Server 2022 load and unload device drivers user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Load and unload device drivers" user right allows a user to load device drivers dynamically on a system. This could be used by an attacker to install malicious code.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true (Load and unload device drivers - Administrators)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25349900 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:Load and unload device drivers - Administrators
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000009 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment:Administrators
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25349900 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Load and unload device drivers
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', seloaddriverprivilege must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • seloaddriverprivilege equals '1' for:
                          • seloaddriverprivilege equals '0' for NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Authenticated Users, RDS Endpoint Servers, Guests, Users, Performance Log Users, Guest, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, IUSR, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, Remote Management Users, TERMINAL SERVER USER, DIALUP, ANONYMOUS LOGON, DefaultAccount, Remote Desktop Users, Distributed COM Users, SYSTEM, NETWORK, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, PROXY, Backup Operators, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, Replicator, Access Control Assistance Operators, Hyper-V Administrators, Performance Monitor Users, Storage Replica Administrators, Everyone, WDAGUtilityAccount, RDS Management Servers, Event Log Readers, SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, Network Configuration Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Administrator, Device Owners, Cryptographic Operators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, Print Operators, IIS_IUSRS, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, System Managed Accounts Group, INTERACTIVE, CREATOR OWNER, RDS Remote Access Servers, OpenSSH Users, User Mode Hardware Operators, NETWORK SERVICE

                          V-254506 - Windows Server 2022 lock pages in memory user right must not be assigned to any groups or accounts.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254506r958726_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-UR-000160
                          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
                          CCI-002824 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: SI-16; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: SI-16)
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Lock pages in memory" user right allows physical memory to be assigned to processes, which could cause performance issues or a denial of service.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000324-GPOS-00125, SRG-OS-000433-GPOS-00193
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Lock pages in memory to be defined but containing no entries (blank).
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254506
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-UR-000160 - Windows Server 2022 lock pages in memory user right must not be assigned to any groups or accounts.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          The "Lock pages in memory" user right allows physical memory to be assigned to processes, which could cause performance issues or a denial of service.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000324-GPOS-00125, SRG-OS-000433-GPOS-00193
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true (Lock pages in memory - None)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25350000 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:Lock pages in memory - None
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000008 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment:All security principles
                          Object Requirements:
                          • security_principle must match the pattern '.+'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25350000 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Lock pages in memory
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', selockmemoryprivilege must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • selockmemoryprivilege equals '1' for:
                          • selockmemoryprivilege equals '0' for Access Control Assistance Operators, Administrators, Backup Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, Cryptographic Operators, Device Owners, Distributed COM Users, Event Log Readers, Guests, Hyper-V Administrators, IIS_IUSRS, Network Configuration Operators, OpenSSH Users, Performance Log Users, Performance Monitor Users, Power Users, Print Operators, RDS Endpoint Servers, RDS Management Servers, RDS Remote Access Servers, Remote Desktop Users, Remote Management Users, Replicator, Storage Replica Administrators, System Managed Accounts Group, User Mode Hardware Operators, Users, Administrator, DefaultAccount, Guest, WDAGUtilityAccount, NETWORK SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, SYSTEM, IUSR, TERMINAL SERVER USER, Authenticated Users, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, PROXY, ANONYMOUS LOGON, SERVICE, INTERACTIVE, BATCH, NETWORK, DIALUP, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, CREATOR GROUP, CREATOR OWNER, Everyone, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Window Manager\Window Manager Group

                          V-254507 - Windows Server 2022 manage auditing and security log user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254507r958434_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-UR-000170
                          Identities:CCI-000162 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-9.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-9 a)
                          CCI-000163 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-9.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-9 a)
                          CCI-000164 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-9.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-9; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-9 a)
                          CCI-000171 (NIST SP 800-53: AU-12 b; NIST SP 800-53A: AU-12.1 (iii); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 b)
                          CCI-001914 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AU-12 (3); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AU-12 (3))
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Manage auditing and security log" user right can manage the security log and change auditing configurations. This could be used to clear evidence of tampering.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000057-GPOS-00027, SRG-OS-000058-GPOS-00028, SRG-OS-000059-GPOS-00029, SRG-OS-000063-GPOS-00032, SRG-OS-000337-GPOS-00129
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Manage auditing and security log to include only the following accounts or groups:

                          - Administrators
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254507
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-UR-000170 - Windows Server 2022 manage auditing and security log user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Manage auditing and security log" user right can manage the security log and change auditing configurations. This could be used to clear evidence of tampering.

                          Satisfies: SRG-OS-000057-GPOS-00027, SRG-OS-000058-GPOS-00028, SRG-OS-000059-GPOS-00029, SRG-OS-000063-GPOS-00032, SRG-OS-000337-GPOS-00129
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))

                          V-254508 - Windows Server 2022 modify firmware environment values user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254508r958726_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-UR-000180
                          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Modify firmware environment values" user right can change hardware configuration environment variables. This could result in hardware failures or a denial of service.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Modify firmware environment values to include only the following accounts or groups:

                          - Administrators
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254508
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-UR-000180 - Windows Server 2022 modify firmware environment values user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Modify firmware environment values" user right can change hardware configuration environment variables. This could result in hardware failures or a denial of service.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true (Modify firmware environment values - Administrators)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25350200 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:Modify firmware environment values - Administrators
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000009 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment:Administrators
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25350200 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Modify firmware environment values
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', sesystemenvironmentprivilege must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • sesystemenvironmentprivilege equals '1' for:
                          • sesystemenvironmentprivilege equals '0' for NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Authenticated Users, RDS Endpoint Servers, Guests, Users, Performance Log Users, Guest, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, IUSR, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, Remote Management Users, TERMINAL SERVER USER, DIALUP, ANONYMOUS LOGON, DefaultAccount, Remote Desktop Users, Distributed COM Users, SYSTEM, NETWORK, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, PROXY, Backup Operators, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, Replicator, Access Control Assistance Operators, Hyper-V Administrators, Performance Monitor Users, Storage Replica Administrators, Everyone, WDAGUtilityAccount, RDS Management Servers, Event Log Readers, SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, Network Configuration Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Administrator, Device Owners, Cryptographic Operators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, Print Operators, IIS_IUSRS, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, System Managed Accounts Group, INTERACTIVE, CREATOR OWNER, RDS Remote Access Servers, OpenSSH Users, User Mode Hardware Operators, NETWORK SERVICE

                          V-254509 - Windows Server 2022 perform volume maintenance tasks user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254509r958726_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-UR-000190
                          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Perform volume maintenance tasks" user right can manage volume and disk configurations. This could be used to delete volumes, resulting in data loss or a denial of service.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Perform volume maintenance tasks to include only the following accounts or groups:

                          - Administrators
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254509
                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-UR-000190 - Windows Server 2022 perform volume maintenance tasks user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Perform volume maintenance tasks" user right can manage volume and disk configurations. This could be used to delete volumes, resulting in data loss or a denial of service.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true (Perform volume maintenance tasks - Administrators)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25350300 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:true
                          Title:Perform volume maintenance tasks - Administrators
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000009 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment:Administrators
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25350300 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Perform volume maintenance tasks
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', semanagevolumeprivilege must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • semanagevolumeprivilege equals '1' for:
                          • semanagevolumeprivilege equals '0' for NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Authenticated Users, RDS Endpoint Servers, Guests, Users, Performance Log Users, Guest, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, IUSR, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, Remote Management Users, TERMINAL SERVER USER, DIALUP, ANONYMOUS LOGON, DefaultAccount, Remote Desktop Users, Distributed COM Users, SYSTEM, NETWORK, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, PROXY, Backup Operators, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, Replicator, Access Control Assistance Operators, Hyper-V Administrators, Performance Monitor Users, Storage Replica Administrators, Everyone, WDAGUtilityAccount, RDS Management Servers, Event Log Readers, SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, Network Configuration Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Administrator, Device Owners, Cryptographic Operators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, Print Operators, IIS_IUSRS, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, System Managed Accounts Group, INTERACTIVE, CREATOR OWNER, RDS Remote Access Servers, OpenSSH Users, User Mode Hardware Operators, NETWORK SERVICE

                          V-254510 - Windows Server 2022 profile single process user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254510r958726_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-UR-000200
                          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Profile single process" user right can monitor nonsystem processes performance. An attacker could use this to identify processes to attack.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Profile single process to include only the following accounts or groups:

                          - Administrators
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:

                          Definition Result:true
                          Title:WN22-UR-000200 - Windows Server 2022 profile single process user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Profile single process" user right can monitor nonsystem processes performance. An attacker could use this to identify processes to attack.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • true (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • true (The 'Profile single process' user right is only assigned to the Administrators group.)

                          Result:true
                          Title:The 'Profile single process' user right is only assigned to the Administrators group.

                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).

                          Object Comment:Administrators
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Exclude Items If:

                          State Comment: 
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', seprofilesingleprocessprivilege must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ true ]
                          • seprofilesingleprocessprivilege equals '1' for:
                          • seprofilesingleprocessprivilege equals '0' for NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Authenticated Users, RDS Endpoint Servers, Guests, Users, Performance Log Users, Guest, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, IUSR, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, Remote Management Users, TERMINAL SERVER USER, DIALUP, ANONYMOUS LOGON, DefaultAccount, Remote Desktop Users, Distributed COM Users, SYSTEM, NETWORK, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, PROXY, Backup Operators, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, Replicator, Access Control Assistance Operators, Hyper-V Administrators, Performance Monitor Users, Storage Replica Administrators, Everyone, WDAGUtilityAccount, RDS Management Servers, Event Log Readers, SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, Network Configuration Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Administrator, Device Owners, Cryptographic Operators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, Print Operators, IIS_IUSRS, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, System Managed Accounts Group, INTERACTIVE, CREATOR OWNER, RDS Remote Access Servers, OpenSSH Users, User Mode Hardware Operators, NETWORK SERVICE

                          V-254511 - Windows Server 2022 restore files and directories user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254511r958726_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Fail
                          Version:WN22-UR-000210
                          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Restore files and directories" user right can circumvent file and directory permissions and could allow access to sensitive data. It could also be used to overwrite more current data.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Restore files and directories to include only the following accounts or groups:

                          - Administrators
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Definitions:
                          Definition ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.windows2022:def:254511
                          Definition Result:false
                          Title:WN22-UR-000210 - Windows Server 2022 restore files and directories user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.
                          Description:Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Restore files and directories" user right can circumvent file and directory permissions and could allow access to sensitive data. It could also be used to overwrite more current data.
                          Class:compliance
                          Tests:
                          • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                            • false (All child tests must be true. (AND))
                              • false (Restore files and directories - Administrators)
                          Tests:
                          Test ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:tst:25350500 (accesstoken_test)
                          Result:false
                          Title:Restore files and directories - Administrators
                          Check Existence:One or more collected items must exist.
                          Check:All collected items must match the given state(s).
                          Object ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:obj:20000009 (accesstoken_object)
                          Object Comment:Administrators
                          Object Requirements:
                          • Collect any available items.
                          Exclude Items If:
                          • security_principle equals (case insensitive) 'Administrators'
                          State ID:oval:mil.disa.stig.win:ste:25350500 (accesstoken_state)
                          State Comment:Restore files and directories
                          State Requirements:
                          • check_existence = 'at_least_one_exists', serestoreprivilege must be equal to '0'
                          Collected Item/State Result:
                          [ false ]
                          • serestoreprivilege equals '1' for: Backup Operators
                          • serestoreprivilege equals '0' for NT SERVICE\WdiServiceHost, Authenticated Users, RDS Endpoint Servers, Guests, Users, Performance Log Users, Guest, RESTRICTED SERVICES\ALL RESTRICTED SERVICES, IUSR, CREATOR GROUP SERVER, Remote Management Users, TERMINAL SERVER USER, DIALUP, ANONYMOUS LOGON, DefaultAccount, Remote Desktop Users, Distributed COM Users, SYSTEM, NETWORK, NT SERVICE\ALL SERVICES, PROXY, Power Users, CREATOR GROUP, Replicator, Access Control Assistance Operators, Hyper-V Administrators, Performance Monitor Users, Storage Replica Administrators, Everyone, WDAGUtilityAccount, RDS Management Servers, Event Log Readers, SERVICE, LOCAL SERVICE, Network Configuration Operators, Certificate Service DCOM Access, BATCH, CREATOR OWNER SERVER, Administrator, Device Owners, Cryptographic Operators, ENTERPRISE DOMAIN CONTROLLERS, Print Operators, IIS_IUSRS, Window Manager\Window Manager Group, System Managed Accounts Group, INTERACTIVE, CREATOR OWNER, RDS Remote Access Servers, OpenSSH Users, User Mode Hardware Operators, NETWORK SERVICE

                          V-254512 - Windows Server 2022 take ownership of files or other objects user right must only be assigned to the Administrators group.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254512r958726_rule
                          Test Type:Automated
                          Rule Result:Pass
                          Version:WN22-UR-000220
                          Identities:CCI-002235 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: AC-6 (10); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: AC-6 (10))
                          Description: Inappropriate granting of user rights can provide system, administrative, and other high-level capabilities.

                          Accounts with the "Take ownership of files or other objects" user right can take ownership of objects and make changes.
                          Fix Text:Configure the policy value for Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Local Policies >> User Rights Assignment >> Take ownership of files or other objects to include only the following accounts or groups:

                          Accounts with the "Take ownership of files or other objects" user right can take ownership of objects and make changes.

                          V-254238 - Windows Server 2022 users with Administrative privileges must have separate accounts for administrative duties and normal operational tasks.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254238r991589_rule
                          Test Type:Manual
                          Rule Result:Not Checked
                          Version:WN22-00-000010
                          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
                          Description: Using a privileged account to perform routine functions makes the computer vulnerable to malicious software inadvertently introduced during a session that has been granted full privileges.
                          Fix Text:Ensure each user with administrative privileges has a separate account for user duties and one for privileged duties.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Questionnaires: Description: Using a privileged account to perform routine functions makes the computer vulnerable to malicious software inadvertently introduced during a session that has been granted full privileges.
                          Questionnaire ID:ocil:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:questionnaire:1
                          Result:NOT_TESTED
                          Title:Windows Server 2022 users with Administrative privileges must have separate accounts for administrative duties and normal operational tasks.
                          Criteria:
                          • NOT_TESTED (All child tests must be true.)
                            • NOT_TESTED (CAT II, V-254238, SV-254238r991589, SRG-OS-000480-GPOS-00227)
                          Criteria:
                          Title:CAT II, V-254238, SV-254238r991589, SRG-OS-000480-GPOS-00227
                          Test Action ID:ocil:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:testaction:1
                          Question:Verify each user with administrative privileges has been assigned a unique administrative account separate from their standard user account.

                          If users with administrative privileges do not have separate accounts for administrative functions and standard user functions, this is a finding.

                          References:
                          CCI-000366
                          Result:NOT_TESTED

                          V-254239 - Windows Server 2022 passwords for the built-in Administrator account must be changed at least every 60 days.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254239r1038967_rule
                          Test Type:Manual
                          Rule Result:Not Checked
                          Version:WN22-00-000020
                          Identities:CCI-004066 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (1) (h))
                          CCI-000199 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (d); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (v); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (d))
                          CCI-000199 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (d); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (v); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (d))
                          Description: The longer a password is in use, the greater the opportunity for someone to gain unauthorized knowledge of the password. The built-in Administrator account is not generally used and its password may not be changed as frequently as necessary. Changing the password for the built-in Administrator account on a regular basis will limit its exposure.

                          Windows LAPS must be used to change the built-in Administrator account password.
                          Fix Text:Change the enabled local Administrator account password at least every 60 days. Windows LAPS must be used to change the built-in Administrator account password. Domain-joined systems can configure this to occur more frequently. LAPS will change the password every 30 days by default.

                          More information is available at:
                          https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/t5/windows-it-pro-blog/by-popular-demand-windows-laps-available-now/ba-p/3788747
                          https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows-server/identity/laps/laps-overview#windows-laps-supported-platforms-and-azure-ad-laps-preview-status
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Questionnaires: Description: The longer a password is in use, the greater the opportunity for someone to gain unauthorized knowledge of the password. The built-in Administrator account is not generally used and its password may not be changed as frequently as necessary. Changing the password for the built-in Administrator account on a regular basis will limit its exposure. Windows LAPS must be used to change the built-in Administrator account password.
                          Questionnaire ID:ocil:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:questionnaire:21
                          Result:NOT_TESTED
                          Title:Windows Server 2022 passwords for the built-in Administrator account must be changed at least every 60 days.
                          Criteria:
                          • NOT_TESTED (All child tests must be true.)
                            • NOT_TESTED (CAT II, V-254239, SV-254239r1038967, SRG-OS-000076-GPOS-00044)
                          Criteria:
                          Title:CAT II, V-254239, SV-254239r1038967, SRG-OS-000076-GPOS-00044
                          Test Action ID:ocil:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:testaction:21
                          Question:If there are no enabled local Administrator accounts, this is Not Applicable.

                          Review the password last set date for the enabled local Administrator account.

                          On the stand alone or domain-joined workstation:

                          Open "PowerShell".

                          Enter "Get-LocalUser -Name * | Select-Object *".

                          If the "PasswordLastSet" date is greater than "60" days old for the local Administrator account for administering the computer/domain, this is a finding.

                          Verify LAPS is configured and operational.

                          Navigate to Local Computer Policy >> Computer Configuration >> Administrative Templates >> System >> LAPS >> Password Settings >> Set to enabled. Password Complexity, large letters + small letters + numbers + special, Password Length 14, Password Age 60. If not configured as shown, this is a finding.

                          Verify LAPS Operational logs >> Event Viewer >> Applications and Services Logs >> Microsoft >> Windows >> LAPS >> Operational. Verify LAPS policy process is completing. If it is not, this is a finding.

                          References:
                          CCI-004066
                          CCI-000199
                          CCI-000199
                          Result:NOT_TESTED

                          V-254241 - Windows Server 2022 members of the Backup Operators group must have separate accounts for backup duties and normal operational tasks.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254241r991589_rule
                          Test Type:Manual
                          Rule Result:Not Checked
                          Version:WN22-00-000040
                          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
                          Description: Backup Operators are able to read and write to any file in the system, regardless of the rights assigned to it. Backup and restore rights permit users to circumvent the file access restrictions present on NTFS disk drives for backup and restore purposes. Members of the Backup Operators group must have separate logon accounts for performing backup duties.
                          Fix Text:Ensure each member of the Backup Operators group has separate accounts for backup functions and standard user functions.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Questionnaires: Description: Backup Operators are able to read and write to any file in the system, regardless of the rights assigned to it. Backup and restore rights permit users to circumvent the file access restrictions present on NTFS disk drives for backup and restore purposes. Members of the Backup Operators group must have separate logon accounts for performing backup duties.
                          Questionnaire ID:ocil:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:questionnaire:61
                          Result:NOT_TESTED
                          Title:Windows Server 2022 members of the Backup Operators group must have separate accounts for backup duties and normal operational tasks.
                          Criteria:
                          • NOT_TESTED (All child tests must be true.)
                            • NOT_TESTED (CAT II, V-254241, SV-254241r991589, SRG-OS-000480-GPOS-00227)
                          Criteria:
                          Title:CAT II, V-254241, SV-254241r991589, SRG-OS-000480-GPOS-00227
                          Test Action ID:ocil:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:testaction:61
                          Question:If no accounts are members of the Backup Operators group, this is NA.

                          Verify users with accounts in the Backup Operators group have a separate user account for backup functions and for performing normal user tasks.

                          If users with accounts in the Backup Operators group do not have separate accounts for backup functions and standard user functions, this is a finding.

                          References:
                          CCI-000366
                          Result:NOT_TESTED

                          V-254242 - Windows Server 2022 manually managed application account passwords must be at least 14 characters in length.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254242r1016472_rule
                          Test Type:Manual
                          Rule Result:Not Checked
                          Version:WN22-00-000050
                          Identities:CCI-004066 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-5 (1) (h))
                          CCI-000205 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (a); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (i); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (a))
                          CCI-000205 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-5 (1) (a); NIST SP 800-53A: IA-5 (1).1 (i); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-5 (1) (a))
                          Description: Application/service account passwords must be of sufficient length to prevent being easily cracked. Application/service accounts that are manually managed must have passwords at least 14 characters in length.
                          Fix Text:Establish a policy that requires application/service account passwords that are manually managed to be at least 14 characters in length. Ensure the policy is enforced.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Questionnaires: Description: Application/service account passwords must be of sufficient length to prevent being easily cracked. Application/service accounts that are manually managed must have passwords at least 14 characters in length.
                          Questionnaire ID:ocil:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:questionnaire:81
                          Result:NOT_TESTED
                          Title:Windows Server 2022 manually managed application account passwords must be at least 14 characters in length.
                          Criteria:
                          • NOT_TESTED (All child tests must be true.)
                            • NOT_TESTED (CAT II, V-254242, SV-254242r1016472, SRG-OS-000078-GPOS-00046)
                          Criteria:
                          Title:CAT II, V-254242, SV-254242r1016472, SRG-OS-000078-GPOS-00046
                          Test Action ID:ocil:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:testaction:81
                          Question:Determine if manually managed application/service accounts exist. If none exist, this is NA.

                          Verify the organization has a policy to ensure passwords for manually managed application/service accounts are at least 14 characters in length.

                          If such a policy does not exist or has not been implemented, this is a finding.

                          References:
                          CCI-004066
                          CCI-000205
                          CCI-000205
                          Result:NOT_TESTED

                          V-254243 - Windows Server 2022 manually managed application account passwords must be changed at least annually or when a system administrator with knowledge of the password leaves the organization.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254243r991589_rule
                          Test Type:Manual
                          Rule Result:Not Checked
                          Version:WN22-00-000060
                          Identities:CCI-000366 (NIST SP 800-53: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53A: CM-6.1 (iv); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-6 b; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-6 b)
                          Description: Setting application account passwords to expire may cause applications to stop functioning. However, not changing them on a regular basis exposes them to attack. If managed service accounts are used, this alleviates the need to manually change application account passwords.
                          Fix Text:Change passwords for manually managed application/service accounts at least annually or when an administrator with knowledge of the password leaves the organization.

                          It is recommended that system-managed service accounts be used whenever possible.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Questionnaires: Description: Setting application account passwords to expire may cause applications to stop functioning. However, not changing them on a regular basis exposes them to attack. If managed service accounts are used, this alleviates the need to manually change application account passwords.
                          Questionnaire ID:ocil:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:questionnaire:101
                          Result:NOT_TESTED
                          Title:Windows Server 2022 manually managed application account passwords must be changed at least annually or when a system administrator with knowledge of the password leaves the organization.
                          Criteria:
                          • NOT_TESTED (All child tests must be true.)
                            • NOT_TESTED (CAT II, V-254243, SV-254243r991589, SRG-OS-000480-GPOS-00227)
                          Criteria:
                          Title:CAT II, V-254243, SV-254243r991589, SRG-OS-000480-GPOS-00227
                          Test Action ID:ocil:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:testaction:101
                          Question:Determine if manually managed application/service accounts exist. If none exist, this is NA.

                          If passwords for manually managed application/service accounts are not changed at least annually or when an administrator with knowledge of the password leaves the organization, this is a finding.

                          Identify manually managed application/service accounts.

                          To determine the date a password was last changed:

                          Domain controllers:

                          Open "PowerShell".

                          Enter "Get-AdUser -Identity [application account name] -Properties PasswordLastSet | FT Name, PasswordLastSet", where [application account name] is the name of the manually managed application/service account.

                          If the "PasswordLastSet" date is more than one year old, this is a finding.

                          Member servers and standalone or nondomain-joined systems:

                          Open "Command Prompt".

                          Enter 'Net User [application account name] | Find /i "Password Last Set"', where [application account name] is the name of the manually managed application/service account.

                          If the "Password Last Set" date is more than one year old, this is a finding.

                          References:
                          CCI-000366
                          Result:NOT_TESTED

                          V-254244 - Windows Server 2022 shared user accounts must not be permitted.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254244r958482_rule
                          Test Type:Manual
                          Rule Result:Not Checked
                          Version:WN22-00-000070
                          Identities:CCI-000764 (NIST SP 800-53: IA-2; NIST SP 800-53A: IA-2.1; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: IA-2; NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: IA-2)
                          Description: Shared accounts (accounts where two or more people log on with the same user identification) do not provide adequate identification and authentication. There is no way to provide for nonrepudiation or individual accountability for system access and resource usage.
                          Fix Text:Remove unapproved shared accounts from the system.

                          Document required shared accounts with the ISSO. Documentation must include the reason for the account, who has access to the account, and how the risk of using the shared account is mitigated to include monitoring account activity.
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Questionnaires: Description: Shared accounts (accounts where two or more people log on with the same user identification) do not provide adequate identification and authentication. There is no way to provide for nonrepudiation or individual accountability for system access and resource usage.
                          Questionnaire ID:ocil:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:questionnaire:121
                          Result:NOT_TESTED
                          Title:Windows Server 2022 shared user accounts must not be permitted.
                          Criteria:
                          • NOT_TESTED (All child tests must be true.)
                            • NOT_TESTED (CAT II, V-254244, SV-254244r958482, SRG-OS-000104-GPOS-00051)
                          Criteria:
                          Title:CAT II, V-254244, SV-254244r958482, SRG-OS-000104-GPOS-00051
                          Test Action ID:ocil:navy.navwar.niwcatlantic.scc.windows2022:testaction:121
                          Question:Determine whether any shared accounts exist. If no shared accounts exist, this is NA.

                          Shared accounts, such as required by an application, may be approved by the organization. This must be documented with the Information System Security Officer (ISSO). Documentation must include the reason for the account, who has access to the account, and how the risk of using the shared account is mitigated to include monitoring account activity.

                          If unapproved shared accounts exist, this is a finding.

                          References:
                          CCI-000764
                          Result:NOT_TESTED

                          V-254245 - Windows Server 2022 must employ a deny-all, permit-by-exception policy to allow the execution of authorized software programs.

                          Rule ID:xccdf_mil.disa.stig_rule_SV-254245r958808_rule
                          Test Type:Manual
                          Rule Result:Not Checked
                          Version:WN22-00-000080
                          Identities:CCI-001774 (NIST SP 800-53 Rev 4: CM-7 (5) (b); NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5: CM-7 (5) (b))
                          Description: Using an allowlist provides a configuration management method to allow the execution of only authorized software. Using only authorized software decreases risk by limiting the number of potential vulnerabilities.

                          The organization must identify authorized software programs and only permit execution of authorized software. The process used to identify software programs that are authorized to execute on organizational information systems is commonly referred to as allowlisting.
                          Fix Text:Configure an application allowlisting program to employ a deny-all, permit-by-exception policy to allow the execution of authorized software programs.

                          Configuration of allowlisting applications will vary by the program. AppLocker is an allowlisting application built in to Windows Server.

                          If AppLocker is used, it is configured through group policy in Computer Configuration >> Windows Settings >> Security Settings >> Application Control Policies >> AppLocker.

                          Implementation guidance for AppLocker is available at the following link:

                          https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/security/threat-protection/windows-defender-application-control/applocker/applocker-policies-deployment-guide
                          Severity:medium
                          Weight:10.0
                          Reference:
                          Title:DPMS Target Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Publisher:DISA
                          Type:DPMS Target
                          Subject:Microsoft Windows Server 2022
                          Identifier:5485
                          Questionnaires: Description: Using an allowlist provides a configuration management method to allow the execution of only authorized software. Using only authorized software decreases risk by limiting the number of potential vulnerabilities.
